programme.title: Crackerjack!
programme.desc: Sam and Mark host a bumper edition of filled with festive fun. Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt play an extra-messy game of Splatterjack, and there is also music from Becky Hill

programme.title: Nativity! (2009)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher is given the job of directing a nativity play and is determined to outdo a rival from a nearby private school. When he dishonestly claims his ex-girlfriend is a Hollywood producer and is coming to see the show with an eye on making a film, he finds himself becoming a local celebrity. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman and Ashley Jensen

programme.title: Donkey's Caroling Christmas-tacular
programme.desc: Animated short following the efforts of lovable Shrek character Donkey, who tries to get his friends in a festive mood by singing a series of Christmas songs. Featuring the voices of Eddie Murphy, Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: Highlights of Ben, Kate and Jean's week at Longleat Safari Park during the festive period, including the surprise birth of a baby aardvark, and a Christmas tree arriving in the elephant house. Elsewhere, keepers face a tough decision as they try to forge a new relationship with cheetahs Carl and Wilma

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: Merry Madagascar
programme.desc: The animals deliver Santa's presents after he crash-lands on the island, but they plan to use the good deed as an excuse to get back to New York City. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded festive edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Dianne Buswell, Max George, Sally Phillips, Milton Jones, John Barnes MBE, Dave Beasant, Jodie Kidd and Lisa Snowdon. Plus, a special performance from Paul Heaton and Jacqui Abbott

programme.title: Mary Berry Saves Christmas
programme.desc: Mary comes to the aid of three members of the public who claim to be hopeless in the kitchen. She gives them a crash course in creating a surprise festive feast for their families, with simple recipes that capture all of the essence and flavours of the season. Helping out in the kitchen will be celebrity sous chefs Jay Blades, Tom Allen and Angela Scanlon

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory in time for Christmas. Among the items is a Victorian version of a jukebox called a Polyphon, that has rusted into silence. It moves sisters Ann and Kathryn to tears when they hear it play the family's favourite carol for the first time in decades

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas comes to the haunted household of Button House in this festive special, and Alison and Mike prepare to host a family Christmas for the first time. Alison finds her phantom housemates less than filled with festive cheer, and learns that Christmas is never easy when you're dead. Mike comes up with a dubious plan to get his parents into the holiday spirit, while Julian is confronted by a new arrival, who forces him to relive memories of his life and come to terms with his past misdeeds

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: In this festive special, John Torode and Gregg Wallace invite some of the most memorable celebrities from series past - Craig Revel Horwood, Amar Latif, Crissy Rock and Spencer Matthews - back to the MasterChef kitchen. The foursome compete against each other in a series of challenges in the hopes of taking home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: In this festive special, Gray is looking forward to the Butterchurn Crescent Christmas lights display, which he hopes will brighten up the end of a difficult year - only for the event to be cancelled due to expense. Having landed a lucrative new contract at work, he takes it upon himself to save the lights - and Christmas

programme.title: Dolly Parton's Christmas Concert
programme.desc: Dolly Parton performs a Christmas concert featuring a host of her favourite festive songs

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. A compilation of the 2020 editions of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the 15th round of fixtures and look ahead to forthcoming contests

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts the game show, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda (2008)
programme.desc: A hero is needed to defend a peaceful valley community from attack by a rampaging snow leopard. The unlikely candidate for the job is a lazy panda, who enlists the aid of a legendary team of animal fighters and their wise master to teach him the martial arts skills he needs to fulfil his destiny. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jack Black, Dustin Hoffman, Angelina Jolie, Ian McShane and Jackie Chan

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda Holiday
programme.desc: Seasonal spin-off from the popular animated film. Po looks forward to helping his father prepare for the Winter Feast - until Shifu reveals this year he must take charge of hosting the celebrations himself

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 3 (2016)
programme.desc: The clumsy hero is reunited with his long-lost father, whose help he will need when a supernatural foe threatens all of China. Father and son travel to a secret village populated by other pandas, and must train them in martial arts so they can defend their home. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black, Bryan Cranston, Angelina Jolie and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: Moana (2016)
programme.desc: To save her island home from a terrible curse, a brave Polynesian teenager must set sail and journey into the great blue yonder with the aid of a legendary hero. Disney animation, with the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Auli'i Cravalho, Rachel House and Temuera Morrison

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: Mackenzie Crook returns as the walking, talking scarecrow of Scatterbrook Farm. When he and his teenage friends Susan and John visit a scrapyard, Worzel hears the grumbling of a foul-mouthed old friend - carved ship's figurehead Saucy Nancy, who has been languishing there for years and longs to return to the sea. Worzel and the kids promise to get her there - but will her ship be waiting? Family adventure, with Shirley Henderson, Vanessa Redgrave, India Brown and Thierry Wickens

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood teaming up with documentary-maker - and Strictly winner - Stacey Dooley to take on the Wall. Their aim is to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Joe Lycett, Joe Swash and Ruth Madeley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for this festive edition of the comedy game show. Did one of the panellists really program a satnav so their mother's trip to Lakeside ended up in the Lake District? And would one of the captains really hold up a train to get his daughter's cuddly toy back? Or were they just festive fibs? Rob Brydon hosts the fun

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy is speechless as Honey and Jay try to explain what he saw and storms out. Linda is desperate to get through to Mick and convince him to fight for her, but he fails to respond. However, when a distraught Tina comes looking for him, he finally snaps into action to help his sister. Ian realises just how horrible he has been and starts making amends - beginning with Peter and Bobby. But as the Albert Square residents gather to celebrate Christmas Eve, he finds himself targeted and terrified for his life yet again

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Another chance to see last year's festive edition of the comedy. The Shipmans travel to Wales to spend Christmas with the Wests, but for Gavin and Stacey, parenthood continues to present them with several challenges, and their 'baby' is now nine. Smithy also continues to commit to his fatherly role, making the journey to Barry at weekends and every second Wednesday to see 12-year-old Neil the Baby. Nessa has adopted her own style of mothering, while Pam, Mick and Gwen have become the epitome of doting grandparents. On Christmas Eve, Dawn makes a shocking discovery about Pete that jeopardises their marriage - again - while a visit to the pub becomes an eye-opener in more ways than one. Starring Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones

programme.title: Miranda's Games with Showbiz Names
programme.desc: Miranda Hart hosts this entertainment show in which three celebrity duos join her from the comfort of their own homes to compete in a series of riotous Christmas games. Those taking part are Shirley Ballas and actor boyfriend Danny Taylor, Robbie Williams and drummer Karl Brazil, and Jermaine Jenas and wife Ellie, while Shirley's fellow Strictly judge Craig Revel Horwood also puts in an appearance

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: This year marks Have I Got News for You's 30th anniversary. BBC One celebrates the past three decades with memories of the greatest moments, remembering the battles, the bust-ups, the guest hosts, seven prime ministers and five presidents - and not forgetting the tub of lard. Contributors include Paul Merton, Ian Hislop, Germaine Greer, Gary Lineker, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Ken Livingstone and Alexander Armstrong

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ronnie Barker's final screen appearance, originally aired two months after his death in 2005, in which he and Ronnie Corbett choose their favourite sketches, songs and monologues from their festive shows, including the Milkman's Christmas Message, Christmas Day in the Yukon and an interpretation of Alice Through the Looking Glass. Katie Melua sings Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, before the duo sign off for the very last time

programme.title: Midnight Mass - BBC
programme.desc: Live service from the Roman Catholic cathedral of St Peter and St Paul in Bristol. The service is led by the Bishop of Clifton, the Rt Rev Declan Lang, and the preacher is Canon Bosco MacDonald. The Cathedral's Schola Cantorum, conducted by Richard Jeffrey-Gray, sing Christmas favourites including Silent Night and Hark! The Herald Angels Sing

programme.title: I Give It a Year (2013)
programme.desc: A couple marry after a whirlwind romance, but family and friends have doubts about the relationship. As their first anniversary approaches, they both begin to wonder if they are right for each other, especially when faced with more appealing alternative partners. Romantic comedy, with Rose Byrne, Rafe Spall, Anna Faris and Simon Baker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Gruffalo
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book. It tells the tale of a little mouse who takes a walk through the woods in search of a nut, only to encounter a fox, an owl and a snake who all want to eat him. To escape their clutches, the quick-witted rodent creates an imaginary monster to scare away his attackers - but it turns out to be all too real. Robbie Coltrane heads the voice cast as the Gruffalo, with James Corden as the Mouse and Rob Brydon, John Hurt and Tom Wilkinson as the predators

programme.title: The Gruffalo's Child
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the sequel to Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book, reuniting all the voice cast from the original. The Gruffalo has raised his daughter on tales of the big, bad mouse, the only creature in the forest he is frightened of. Despite her father's warnings, she sets off in search of this scary creature, along the way encountering the snake, owl and the fox. But when she still has no luck finding the evil rodent, she begins to think he does not exist after all. Robbie Coltrane, James Corden and Rob Brydon lend their voices, with Shirley Henderson as Gruffalo Jr

programme.title: Lou (2017)
programme.desc: Oscar-nominated Pixar animation about a pile of lost and found items coming together to teach a toy-stealing schoolyard bully a valuable lesson

programme.title: Christmas Day Eucharist
programme.desc: Senior Pastor, Bishop Paul McCalla, and Peter Sutcliffe, Senior Pastor at the Nechells Outreach Centre lead a festive act of worship, live from the Church of God of Prophecy in Birmingham. The gospel-themed affair features a small group of singers and musicians, all socially distanced in compliance with Covid regulations. Music includes some of the best-loved carols, including Joy to the World, Hark! The Herald Angels Sing and Mary Did You Know?

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Keen but accident-prone dragon Zog gets himself into mischief while learning how to fly, roar, breathe fire and fight knights in his first four years at Dragon School. Each year he meets a kindly young girl who patches up his bumps and bruises, but can she help him with his trickiest challenge yet? It's year five and he has to capture a princess. Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's book, featuring the voices of Lenny Henry, Tracey Ullman, Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Kit Harington

programme.title: Top of the Pops Christmas Special
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo review the hottest stars and biggest songs of the last 12 months from a very different year in music. Featuring performances from AJ Tracey and Aitch, Becky Hill, Celeste, Clean Bandit and Mabel, Ella Henderson, Jamie Cullum, Joel Corry and MNEK, and KSI featuring Craig David and Digital Farm Animals. Plus, the Christmas number one is revealed

programme.title: Inner Workings (2016)
programme.desc: Award-winning Animated Disney short that brings to life the constant internal struggle between head and heart. Paul works in an office at Boring, Boring and Glum. Inside him, his brain struggles to motivate him to work and battles against his heart's desire to break out of the dull routine and have some fun instead

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: Young Dug's sheltered life with his Stone Age tribe is shattered by bronze-mining invaders led by greedy Lord Nooth. To win his home back, desperate Dug gambles on challenging these more sophisticated folk in a contest they love, but he only knows from rock paintings - football. Director Nick Park's animated adventure, with the voices of Eddie Redmayne, Timothy Spall, Rob Brydon, Miriam Margolyes and Johnny Vegas

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty's annual address to the nation and the people of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Coco (2017)
programme.desc: A Mexican boy longs to be a guitarist, despite his family hating music. During the Day of the Dead festivities, he gets transported to the land of the dead, and with the help of a cunning skeleton, hopes to find his ancestor and learn the secrets of his family's history. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Anthony Gonzalez and Gael Garcia Bernal

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A chance to see the 25 most memorable dances of all time, voted for by the public, and a brand-new Christmas-themed group number. Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman are also joined by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood, the show's regular professionals and some very special celebrity guests to take a look back at some of the most iconic moments ever to grace the Strictly ballroom. From crowd favourites to perfect 40s and trophy-winning performances, this show has everything necessary to add a little Strictly sparkle to Christmas Day

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a special celebrity edition of the game show, with contestants Holly Willoughby, Alan Carr and Oti Mabuse competing to win a cash prize for the charity of their choice. Helping the contestants to win as much money as possible will be a panel of seven celebrity experts, each with their own Christmas specialist subject. Alexander Armstrong, Christopher Biggins, Clare Balding, Howard Donald, the Rev Kate Bottley, Olly Smith and Ainsley Harriott will help to answer questions on topics ranging from Christmas dinner to panto

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh is in charge as the popular game show returns for a one-off festive special. Members of a panel comprising comedian Jimmy Carr, pop star Danny Jones, actress Emilia Fox, boxer Amir Khan, comedian Sue Perkins and presenter Anita Rani do their best to fill in the blanks to help the contestants win a selection of prizes - or at the very least go home with the famous Blankety Blank chequebook and pen - before the winner goes through to the supermatch game to play for the jackpot

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: It's December 1965, and everyone at Nonnatus House is looking forward to the festive celebrations, but nothing seems to go quite to plan. Sister Monica Joan is rushed to hospital, and Trixie is incensed that her dress allowance has been replaced with a marriage bureau consultation. Elsewhere, the circus arrives in Poplar, leading to an exciting adventure for Nurse Phyllis Crane. Seasonal instalment of the hit drama, starring Jenny Agutter, Linda Bassett, Judy Parfitt, Helen George, Peter Davison and featuring the voice of Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian identifies his would-be killer and the police are quick to make an arrest. But have they got the right person? Denise comes face to face with a ghost from the past, and Stacey is surprised to receive an expensive gift from Martin. Mick spends an awkward Christmas lunch with Tina and Shirley at Gray's house

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Brendan O'Carroll's indomitable matriarch returns for the first of two festive specials, which finds the family adjusting to the new normal. Buster and Dermot have got new jobs raising awareness of the dangers of coronavirus, while a flasher is terrorising the neighbourhood. Winnie and Agnes are more concerned with winning the chance to perform an alternative Queen's speech - and may be able to provide Father Damien with some tips on getting his message across

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The villagers celebrate Christmas, and vicar Geraldine learns there is a heavy price to be paid for her kindness when she agrees to attend three slap-up lunches and takes part in a Brussels sprout-eating competition. Festive special from 1996, guest starring Peter Capaldi and Mel Giedroyc

programme.title: On Christmas Night
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo narrates the story of the first Christmas from Luke's Gospel, when angels appeared to shepherds to proclaim the news of Jesus's birth

programme.title: Paul McCartney at The Cavern Club
programme.desc: The former Beatle's barnstorming 2018 performance at Liverpool's Cavern Club, where his career with the band started out. Around 250 people crammed into the sweaty venue to hear the 28-song set featuring classics he performed when the Beatles started out in the early 1960s, plus songs from his then-new solo album, Egypt Station

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams set off from Panama City across the Caribbean Sea, but they face an unplanned obstacle when a state of emergency is declared in Quito, Ecuador - the destination of their next checkpoint. The teams are forced to change route in mid-race and head into the remote Colombian desert. Rob and Jen make use of night buses to get ahead, Emon and Jamiul raise some money by teaching scuba diving, and Sam struggles with the pressure and needs a break

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Buchanan joins the Pazhetnovs, a pioneering family of biologists in Russia as they attempt to rehab grizzly bear cub orphans. There are more than 100,000 grizzlies in Russia and as humans encroach further into this vast wilderness, conflict is increasing. The Pazhetnovs have been rescuing orphaned cubs for nearly three decades and have successfully returned more than 200 of them back to the wild. Gordon arrives in February when six cubs have just been rescued. They are just five weeks old and each weigh less than a bag of sugar

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas makes an appeal on behalf of Winston's Wish, a charity providing support for bereaved children and their families

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate Bargain Hunt's 20th birthday, Christina Trevanion tours the UK looking back at what our teams have bought over the last two decades

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a festive feast with a Nordic theme for a group of friends. After a wintery walk through the woods in which she divulges her plans, Nigella gets to work on pickled red cabbage and cucumber salad, no knead black bread, smoked salmon, and Norwegian Pork ribs. A glass of pomegranate fizz accompanies the meal which is followed by the traditional Dutch New Year's Eve treat of doughnuts lightly dusted in icing sugar

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Young Mr Grace celebrates his birthday, so the staff decide to organise a cabaret in his honour. Another chance to see the 1978 Christmas special of the comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon challenges a rival regiment to a gruelling duel, involving map-reading, initiative tests, river-crossing and field-fighting, to prove they are fitter and better trained than any other troop. Christmas special of the comedy with the bumbling Home Guard of Walmington-on-Sea from 1971, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Ian Lavender

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: The Magical World of Julia Donaldson
programme.desc: Celebration of the life and work of the bestselling children's author, with animated sequences by long-term illustrator Axel Scheffler bringing her story to life. The film reveals the surprising stories behind iconic characters like the Gruffalo, Zog and Stick Man, and explores how her simple but poignant handling of themes of love, loss, fear and tyranny appeal to children and adults alike

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams of famous alumni from Courtauld Institute of Art and Goldsmiths, University of London, compete for a place in the semi-finals. The Courtauld team, which feature artist Jeremy Deller and writer and poet Lavinia Greenlaw, go up against the team from Goldsmiths, which features Hairy Biker Dave Myers and author Helen Cross

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Christmas special of the sitcom in which Amanda organises her annual festive soiree, complete with a strict code of conduct on when and where to drink mulled wine and an evil Santa. Meg is the only other mother to earn an invite, but Julia, Kevin and Liz all tag along anyway. For Julia, the party is a welcome distraction from hosting her in-laws, while Liz is trying to ignore Christmas altogether and Kevin is single-handedly stuffing, wrapping, roasting, parboiling, laying up, washing up and decorating everything he can lay his hands on

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a festive edition of the panel game, joined by Chris McCausland, Justin Moorhouse, Holly Walsh and regular Alan Davies. The panel is posed questions on topics like how to get a reindeer into a submarine, why the children of Dijon rebelled against Santa Claus and what the immaculate conception really means. Along the way, the show sets two Christmas-themed world records

programme.title: Phantom Thread (2017)
programme.desc: Celebrated British dressmaker Reynolds Woodcock falls in love with Alma, a shy but strong-willed waitress who refuses to conform to his meticulous way of living. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning drama, starring Daniel Day-Lewis in his final role before retirement, alongside Vicky Krieps and Lesley Manville

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by two guests to take a look at the year's highlights

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Coronavirus is spreading and the British public are told to stay away from pubs. Tom and the landlords he has been supporting face a moral dilemma. Do they keep trying to trade or do they close their doors to fight the virus? However, on March 20 the decision is taken out of their hands when the Prime Minister tells all pubs to close until further notice. Tom started this journey to save four pubs from going under, now every pub in Britain, his own included, find themselves in a fight for survival

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: During the hottest time of year, the waterhole becomes busier in the cool of night. Using remote cameras, the team uncovers the new dynamics of the period following sunset. Hyena move into the area, but they are so elusive that it is hard to know how big the clan is. Their arrival has a dramatic effect on other predators in the area, and no lions or leopards are spotted while they are around. Ella Al-Shamahi discovers how the increased footfall has changed the shape of the waterhole and how, as temperatures rise, the waterhole residents' behaviour is also changing

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second group of four face two tough tests in a bid to make it through to finals week. First they receive a masterclass with Michelin-starred chef Andrew Wong, who demonstrates two dishes - dim sum and crispy chilli beef. They must then produce two dishes, emulating Andrew's techniques, with the judges' favourite receiving a pass through to next week. the remaining three must serve up a two-course signature menu, after which Monica Galetti, Gregg Wallace and Marcus Wareing will decide who else deserves a place in the final

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. With only 10 weeks before they are due to be released, Gordon Buchanan begins training the cubs for their new life in the Russian wilderness. They learn survival skills and spend time exploring a special wilderness training zone just outside the enclosure where they can experience the wild. However, little cub Pasha is still struggling, and the team worry he may not make it

programme.title: Scrooge (1970)
programme.desc: Hard-hearted Victorian miser Ebenezer Scrooge refuses to join in with the spirit of the festive season - until a succession of spectral visitors turn up to teach him the error of his ways. Musical adaptation of Charles Dickens' classic A Christmas Carol, starring Albert Finney, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More, Edith Evans and Paddy Stone

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best-loved festive movies

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents a festive edition from Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire. Experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the teams spend £300 on three items with the aim of making a profit at auction in Derbyshire. Plus, Anita joins a band for a festive sing-a-long and looks into the history of Christmas carols

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores unusual and unexplained natural events that occur around the world during winter, enlisting the aid of leading scientists to uncover why these strange events happen. The programme examines unusual weather conditions, including tornadoes made of snow, and animals behaving in unexpected ways, from snowboarding crows to penguins attempting death-defying feats

programme.title: Meet Me in St Louis (1944)
programme.desc: A job offer tempts a banker to consider uprooting his close-knit family and moving to New York. However, his wife and children struggle with the prospect of leaving their friends behind, and are horrified when they realise they might miss out on the 1904 St Louis World's Fair. Meanwhile, teenage daughter Esther faces the abrupt termination of her romance with the boy next door. Musical, starring Judy Garland, Tom Drake and Margaret O'Brien

programme.title: Paddington: The Man Behind the Bear
programme.desc: Following straight on from the premiere of Paddington 2 over on BBC One, this documentary explores where the original stories came from. Creator Michael Bond drew on his wartime memories of evacuee children to shape the story of the little bear from darkest Peru, while his literary agent, who came from a Jewish family who fled the Nazis, inspired Paddington's kindly friend Mr Gruber. Featuring interviews with Michael Bond's family, friends and celebrity admirers, including Stephen Fry, Bernard Cribbins and Heston Blumenthal, and readings by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Christmas Show
programme.desc: Memorable moments from a decade of the comedy duo's Christmas shows, featuring appearances by newsreader Angela Rippon, singer Shirley Bassey and conductor Andre Previn, who all show a different side to their personalities

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reunite behind their famous desk to reminisce about their favourite comedy moments from their long-running TV shows. Again they present a selection of monologues, musical numbers and sketches, including the classic Fork Handles

programme.title: Carols from King's
programme.desc: The traditional celebration of Christmas from the candlelit Chapel of King's College, where the solo chorister singing the opening verse of Once in Royal David's City marks the beginning of Christmas for millions of people around the world. This year there will be no congregation and the choir and the King's Singers will be socially distanced as they sing carols old and new, including the Sussex Carol, In the Bleak Mid-Winter, Still, Still, Still, Candlelight Carol and O Come All Ye Faithful, with readings from the Bible and poetry by Toni Morrison and Howard Thurman by members of King's College

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix for a Christmas treat in his favourite British city, Bristol. First up is a French bistro where they enjoy a stunning cote de boeuf and Michel makes aligot, known as the best mashed potato in the world. The duo then enjoy a swim in one of Britain's oldest lidos, which has an award-winning restaurant serving up a delicious breakfast. Escaping to the countryside, they visit a countryside hotel and restaurant when Fred makes buffalo mozzarella while Michel samples the hotel's own cider, before a return to the city and the multicultural neighbourhood of St Pauls, where they learn to cook Caribbean jerk turkey

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the fourth match of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. The Nottingham team, with explorer Levison Wood and ecologist Helen Roy, fight it out against the Sheffield team, which includes news presenter Kylie Pentelow and Daily Telegraph columnist Michael Deacon

programme.title: Cinderella: A Comic Relief Pantomime for Christmas
programme.desc: The worlds of theatre, film, TV and comedy collide in this at-home pantomime read-through, written by Richard Curtis and the Dawson brothers, aiming to raise money to fight loneliness, hunger and cold at home and abroad. Olivia Colman is the Fairy Godmother, with her Crown co-star Helena Bonham Carter as Devilia. Real-life brother and sister Daisy May and Charlie Cooper are the Evil Stepsisters, Guz Khan takes on Buttons and Tom Hollander is Baron Hardup

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The show reaches its climax as the winners prepare for the ultimate Christmas feast. But when a second wave makes a traditional banquet impossible, Andi Oliver and the winning chefs formulate a plan to make sure they deliver on their promise to celebrate the nation's key workers. If the workers cannot come to the banquet, they will take it to them, hosting six mini-dinners - and giving others a 'banquet-in-a-box' to cook at home

programme.title: Martin's Close
programme.desc: In 1684, John Martin is on trial for his life, but this is not a cut and dried murder case because the young woman he is accused of killing has been seen after her death. Mark Gatiss's adaptation of MR James' ghost story, starring Peter Capaldi, Wilf Scolding, Simon Williams, Sara Crowe, James Holmes and Fisayo Akinade

programme.title: The Dead Room
programme.desc: Ghost story written and directed by Mark Gatiss that is set in the studios of a long-running radio horror series. The presenter of the show (Simon Callow) resents the fact that he must adapt to changing times and tastes, but during an eerie night's work, he begins to suspect that dark forces are at work in the studio and his own past may not be as buried as he expected. With Anjli Mohindra, Susan Penhaligon and Joshua Oakes-Rogers

programme.title: The Lost Ghost Story with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: The actor and writer examines the life and career of author MR James, trying to discover how this devout Anglican bachelor created supernatural tales that continue to chill readers more than a century on. He visits James's childhood home in Suffolk as well as Eton College and King's College, Cambridge, the two institutions where he spent most of his life, and ventures into ancient churches, dark cloisters and echoing libraries along the way as he attempt to uncover the writer's inspirations

programme.title: Ghost Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Professor Philip Goodman, famous for debunking hoaxes and ghost sightings, is given a dossier of three terrifying, unsolvable cases by his hero and role model Charles Cameron, who has been missing, presumed dead, for years. Can Goodman explain what even Cameron could not? Horror anthology, starring Andy Nyman, Martin Freeman and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: Merry Christmas Baby - With Gregory Porter & Friends
programme.desc: The singer presents a seasonal mix of warm and joyful music with a jazz vibe. Special guests including songstress Corinne Bailey Rae and saxophonist Courtney Pine join Gregory for an evening of traditional Christmas classics alongside jazz standards, filmed in front of an audience in Cardiff Bay. The show has all the ingredients of a perfect winter warmer, making it ideal viewing on a mellow Christmas night. Performances include Merry Christmas Baby, Silent Night, Take Me to the Alley and The Christmas Song

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Grace Dent and Nathan Caton to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including Take That's comeback, Daniel Craig's debut as James Bond in Casino Royale and the release of Amy Winehouse's album Back to Black

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Carols from King's
programme.desc: The traditional celebration of Christmas from the candlelit Chapel of King's College, where the solo chorister singing the opening verse of Once in Royal David's City marks the beginning of Christmas for millions of people around the world. This year there will be no congregation and the choir and the King's Singers will be socially distanced as they sing carols old and new, including the Sussex Carol, In the Bleak Mid-Winter, Still, Still, Still, Candlelight Carol and O Come All Ye Faithful, with readings from the Bible and poetry by Toni Morrison and Howard Thurman by members of King's College

programme.title: The Gruffalo & Me: The Remarkable Julia Donaldson
programme.desc: Celebration of the life and work of the bestselling children's author, with animated sequences by long-term illustrator Axel Scheffler bringing her story to life. The film reveals the surprising stories behind iconic characters like the Gruffalo, Zog and Stick Man, and explores how her simple but poignant handling of themes of love, loss, fear and tyranny appeal to children and adults alike

programme.title: Reindeer Family & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels to Finnish Lapland to meet a family from the nomadic Sami culture, who rely on reindeer to help them travel across the frozen wilderness. He learns about the culture's traditional bond with the animals and picks up some tips on surviving during a harsh winter, before he and his reindeer companion undertake a gruelling journey to see the northern lights

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of film and television appearances made by actor and dancer Gene Kelly over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald O'Connor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make 'em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Cue the Queen: Celebrating the Christmas Speech
programme.desc: Kirsty Young takes a look at the story of the Royal Christmas broadcast and how the tradition started by King George V in 1932 has found a place at the heart of Christmas Day. Beginning with tentative steps in the early days of the wireless, then providing reassurance during the uncertainty of the Second World War, it has become the Queen's televised message that loyal subjects around the world know and love

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Christmas Show
programme.desc: Seasonal special first shown in 1975, in which the duo have a memorable meeting with Robin Day, join Pan's People and persuade Diana Rigg to join them in a play 'wot Ernie wrote'. Plus, the classic streaker sketch and Eric's encounter with his arch-enemy Des O'Connor

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Living in reduced circumstances does not blind Audrey to her responsibilities as Christmas approaches at the Grantleigh estate, while a falling out with Richard means the local church is still without a nativity crib. A special festive edition from 1979 of the comedy, starring Penelope Keith, Peter Bowles and Angela Thorne

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty's annual address to the nation and the people of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Matthew Bourne's The Red Shoes
programme.desc: Recording of the award-winning ballet production based on the classic 1948 film. A dancer's creative ambitions place her at the centre of a conflict between two very different men, who both in their own way inspire her passion. Ashley Shaw, Adam Cooper and Dominic North head the cast, while the score incorporates the music of golden-age Hollywood composer Bernard Herrmann, played by the New Adventures Orchestra

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Frazer is unable to hide his delight when Captain Mainwaring's disreputable brother gatecrashes a sherry party for local dignitaries and tries to reclaim a pocket watch his father promised him. Comedy first aired in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe in a dual role, with John Le Mesurier and John Laurie

programme.title: Victoria Wood's All Day Breakfast
programme.desc: The comedian's parody of daytime magazine programmes, with sketches linked by Duncan Preston and Wood as husband-and-wife duo Martin Cumbernauld and Sally Crossthwaite. Characters include fitness fanatic Jolly Polly, agony uncle Gerard and a soap that airs four times per day. With Julie Walters, Celia Imrie, Susie Blake, Alan Rickman, Lill Roughley, Shirley Cain, Richard Lintern and Philip Lowrie

programme.title: Victoria Wood: The Secret List
programme.desc: With a career spanning more than 40 years and crossing every possible genre, Victoria Wood amassed a back catalogue bulging with sublime comedy moments. The first of two documentaries offers a unique opportunity to see the very best bits of Victoria's work that she loved the most, drawing on some of the 20 sketches chosen by her in a notebook discovered after her death. Plus, contributors including Russell T Davies, Ken Loach, Winsome Pinnock, Jane Wymark and Joan Armatrading discuss Victoria's broad catalogue of award-winning comedy. Narrated by Sara Pascoe

programme.title: La La Land (2016)
programme.desc: Jobbing actress Mia and struggling jazz pianist Sebastian fall in love and attempt to realise their respective dreams in Los Angeles - a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. However, keeping the wolf from the door without compromising their beliefs proves harder than expected. Damien Chazelle's Bafta-winning musical, starring Emma Stone, Ryan Gosling and John Legend

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017)
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high-school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Raymond Briggs: Snowmen, Bogeymen & Milkmen
programme.desc: Profile of the author and illustrator, marking the 40th anniversary of the original publication of The Snowman. The programme explores Briggs' other work, including Fungus the Bogeyman, Father Christmas, When the Wind Blows and Ethel & Ernest, and includes animated interludes with artwork by Chris Riddell. Interviews with Briggs and contributions from Andy Serkis, Nick Park, Steve Bell and Posy Simmonds feature throughout. Narrated by Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: More of the renowned duo's favourite madcap moments, including the Squash sketch, Identical Dialogue Party, Real Water, Towels/Tiles and Rhyming Vicar, plus Ronnie Corbett's monologue Green Books and another helping of the Phantom Raspberry Blower. Renee Olstead is the musical guest, performing Midnight at the Oasis

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint snow-covered trees and shrubs dressed in finest lace among the soft glows of winter

programme.title: Sacred Music
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale travels through Italy, Britain, Germany and Austria to explore how the music of Christmas has evolved in response to changing ideas about the Nativity. His journey spans two millennia, from a fragment of papyrus preserving the earliest known piece of Christian music through to the stories behind Hark! The Herald Angels Sing, Silent Night and In the Bleak Midwinter, as well as the work of one of today's most popular Christmas composers, John Rutter. Music is performed by Harry Christophers and his choir The Sixteen

programme.title: Mother Love
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' Bafta-winning 1989 adaptation of Domini Taylor's novel, starring Diana Rigg. Furious that Alex has moved on after the death of his wife, Helena must deal with the next betrayal. With James Wilby and David McCallum

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The arts critic interviews the actress, who originally found fame as Emma Peel in TV series The Avengers, before starring in films including On Her Majesty's Secret Service and The Hospital, as well as developing a reputation as one of Britain's leading stage performers. She discusses her childhood in India, her mixed feelings about being regarded as a sex symbol, and the skills that have enabled her to continue working into her seventies

programme.title: Carole King and Friends at Christmas
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs festive favourites at a concert that also features Sheffield crooner Richard Hawley, retro vocal trio the Puppini Sisters, Brighton group the Mummers, and New York jazz vocalist Gregory Porter. Among the songs on the bill are Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, Silent Night, Let It Snow, White Christmas and The Christmas Song

programme.title: Country Christmas
programme.desc: Festive performances by the biggest names in country music, including Dan + Shay, Darius Rucker and Lindsey Stirling, Florida Georgia Line, Gabby Barrett, Kelsea Ballerini, Lady A, Little Big Town, Tim McGraw and host Thomas Rhett

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint snow-covered trees and shrubs dressed in finest lace among the soft glows of winter

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: In a festive special, James puts together a Hornby Flying Scotsman, his favourite childhood Christmas present, which he was given in 1972 and sent him on to a path of mechanical intrigue and reassembly. Laid out in all its 138 tiny component parts, James lovingly reassembles the train as well as his memories of Christmases and toys past

programme.title: Sacred Music
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale travels through Italy, Britain, Germany and Austria to explore how the music of Christmas has evolved in response to changing ideas about the Nativity. His journey spans two millennia, from a fragment of papyrus preserving the earliest known piece of Christian music through to the stories behind Hark! The Herald Angels Sing, Silent Night and In the Bleak Midwinter, as well as the work of one of today's most popular Christmas composers, John Rutter. Music is performed by Harry Christophers and his choir The Sixteen

programme.title: Fanny Cradock Cooks for Christmas
programme.desc: Fanny Cradock demonstrates two Christmas cakes - traditional black plum cake and a modern 'white' Christmas cake. She also has advice on making almond paste and icing the cake

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross produces a calm winter scene with snowflakes gently tumbling in the silence of the forest

programme.title: Viva Las Vegas (1964)
programme.desc: A charismatic racing driver arrives in the city to prepare for its first grand prix. Unfortunately, his car has no engine, so to make the money to buy one he takes a job as a casino waiter. However, he loses focus when he falls in love, leaving a rival with the means to steal the race and his girl. Musical, with Elvis Presley, Ann-Margret, Cesare Danova and William Demarest

programme.title: Elvis: The Rebirth of the King
programme.desc: When Elvis Presley began performing in Las Vegas in 1969, many believed that was the end of his career, especially after the excitement of his '68 Comeback Special. This documentary follows his time in the gambling capital and argues that it was a musical renaissance for the King, who reached his peak as both as a singer and performer - jumpsuits and all

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Archive performances from bands covering Elvis Presley hits, including Texas, Cher, Fine Young Cannibals, ZZ Top and the Pet Shop Boys

programme.title: Sings Elvis
programme.desc: Compilation of cover versions of Elvis Presley hits, originally shown in 2010 - the year that would have been his 75th birthday. Artists featured include Cliff Richard, John Cale, Paul McCartney and Tom Jones, and Carl Perkins and Mac Davis perform songs they wrote but which Elvis made famous

programme.title: All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride
programme.desc: A two-hour sleigh ride filmed in Karasjok, Norway - 200 miles north of the Arctic Circle - shot from the point of view of a reindeer herder travelling across the frozen wilderness. Following the path of an ancient postal route, the ride captures the traditional world of the Sami people who are indigenous to northern Scandinavia and for whom reindeer herding remains a way of life

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross produces a calm winter scene with snowflakes gently tumbling in the silence of the forest

programme.title: Elvis: The Rebirth of the King
programme.desc: When Elvis Presley began performing in Las Vegas in 1969, many believed that was the end of his career, especially after the excitement of his '68 Comeback Special. This documentary follows his time in the gambling capital and argues that it was a musical renaissance for the King, who reached his peak as both as a singer and performer - jumpsuits and all

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe sifts through the Top of the Pops archives to present a selection of festive performances, including songs by Slade, the Pogues, Elton John, Take That, Coldplay and Abba

programme.title: Royal Opera All Star Gala
programme.desc: At the famous Royal Opera House stage, Antonio Pappano conducts a stellar cast of singers in favourite arias, duets and choruses from operas by Rossini, Donizetti and Puccini, along with the thrilling finale of Bizet's Carmen. Italian baritone Vito Priante offers haircuts as the Barber of Seville and Lisette Oropesa dazzles with her flawless soprano coloratura before being joined by American tenor Charles Castronovo for some heartwarming comedy in Donizetti's The Elixir of Love. Mezzo Aigul Akhmetshina starts out as Cinderella celebrating her good fortune and then magically transforms into femme fatale Carmen for the tragic finale of Bizet's great opera. The drama increases with Kristine Opolais singing the role of doomed diva Tosca, with Canadian baritone Gerald Finley joining her for the searing Te Deum from Puccini's masterpiece. The chorus and orchestra of the Royal Opera House are arrayed throughout the stalls of the auditorium to maintain social distancing. The concert, recorded

programme.title: Maria by Callas (2017)
programme.desc: The life of Maria Callas is explored based on the words and thoughts of the late soprano herself. Tom Volf's documentary, which took four years of painstaking research to assemble, bases its appraisal on the diva's television interviews, from the entries in her diaries and from the letters she sent to her friends. The film reveals the essence of an extraordinary woman, who rose from humble beginnings in New York City to become a glamorous international superstar and one of the greatest artists of all time

programme.title: Florence Foster Jenkins (2016)
programme.desc: A tone deaf socialite imagines she is a great singer, and her conniving yet devoted husband allows her to remain in her fantasy by holding concerts with small, invited audiences. However, reality threatens to intrude when she announces her intention of realising a lifelong dream of performing in New York's Carnegie Hall, recruiting a struggling young pianist to aid her. Fact-based comedy, starring Meryl Streep and Hugh Grant

programme.title: Ice Dream: Lapland's Snow Show
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton reports from Lapland, where some of the biggest names in art and architecture - including Zaha Hadid, Anish Kapoor and Future Systems - gathered in 2004 to produce an extraordinary exhibition of works made from ice and snow

programme.title: Paul McCartney at the Electric Proms
programme.desc: In a performance at the Roundhouse in north London, Paul McCartney and his band feature much-loved Beatles classics, including Magical Mystery Tour and Eleanor Rigby, as well as Wings material and songs from his solo career. McCartney is joined by a special string section for this truly memorable event, which was first aired in 2007 as part of the BBC's Electric Proms

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: When Mr Crosswire gets a robot butler for Father's Day, Muffy fears Bailey may be replaced

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Pidsley the cat is upset when his favourite spot is taken by Bitzer, who gets tucked up inside the house to recover from a cold

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Inept villain the Snowman is fed up with being humiliatingly defeated by Danger Mouse and decides to get revenge by kidnapping Father Christmas. DM and Penfold's attempts to thwart him end in disaster with the villain super-powered by Christmas spirit and Santa's hat. The Snowman then threatens to destroy the world by freezing the sun, leaving DM with no option but to lead an army of elves and a rejuvenated Santa to battle the Snowman and melt his evil plans

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Mobile Unit is called to Odd Squad’s top-secret security facility.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: When Gnasher is confined to bed with a toothache, Dennis creates his own special version of the beanstalk fairy tale to cheer him up - and Dennis and Gnasher climb the beanstalk to a mystical land

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper learns what life would be like if she never joined The Next Step

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Piper struggles to reignite the love of dance in everyone

programme.title: Almost Never
programme.desc: Chloe and Nate's first Xmas is disrupted when Anika receives an unwelcome letter in the post

programme.title: Max and Harvey: FOMO
programme.desc: Max and Harvey bring you up to speed with all things CBBC and more! Packed full of vlogs and funny challenges

programme.title: Donkey's Caroling Christmas-tacular
programme.desc: Animated short following the efforts of lovable Shrek character Donkey, who tries to get his friends in a festive mood by singing a series of Christmas songs. Featuring the voices of Eddie Murphy, Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: Dragons: Gift of the Night Fury
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon, in which the Vikings prepare to celebrate their winter festival, only to find all the winged reptiles have mysteriously disappeared. Hiccup tries to discover where they have gone, and stumbles on a mysterious island. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Scream Street
programme.desc: Luke tries to save the town from the Grumpus and restore a Merry Christmas

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: While fighting over Viking treasure, the Nektons take over the Dark Orca. The pirates in turn take over the Aronnax. Neither side is happy about strangers in their homes, but neither is willing to back down

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: The Nektons explore a floating island and find the final piece of the Ephemychron in a cave. However, the question remains of why the island is moving

programme.title: Joe All Alone
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Joe finds himself left alone while his mum and unpleasant stepdad Dean go on holiday. He befriends Asha, the granddaughter of his neighbour and together they formulate a plan. Adaption of the book by Joanne Nadin, with Daniel Frogson

programme.title: Joe All Alone
programme.desc: Asha persuades Joe to go on a bus ride around London with her and Otis. For the first time in ages, Joe feels free

programme.title: Joe All Alone
programme.desc: Joe is violently sick for two days

programme.title: Joe All Alone
programme.desc: Joe leaves for Paddington Station and is unexpectedly joined by Asha

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: Pressure is on for Ekraj, who has high hopes at the Male Artistic National Championships

programme.title: Katy
programme.desc: With the added pressure of starting a new school, Katy must adjust to her life in a wheelchair, and Izzie is her main carer at home. Will they ever learn to get along?

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Inept villain the Snowman is fed up with being humiliatingly defeated by Danger Mouse and decides to get revenge by kidnapping Father Christmas. DM and Penfold's attempts to thwart him end in disaster with the villain super-powered by Christmas spirit and Santa's hat. The Snowman then threatens to destroy the world by freezing the sun, leaving DM with no option but to lead an army of elves and a rejuvenated Santa to battle the Snowman and melt his evil plans

programme.title: Diddy TV
programme.desc: Diddy TV proudly presents a brand new medieval entertainment show, Strictly Come Lancing. Also featuring a talent show where the judges get their just deserts.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Walter takes over Butch Butcher's sausage-making business.

programme.title: She-Ra
programme.desc: Bow seeks help from his First Ones historian parents to decode the degraded message on his tracker pad.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Mouse is bored of always doing the same things the same way, so Girl suggests he starts thinking outside the box and live his life differently.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Mobile Unit faces a series of odd challenges that only Orla knows how to stop.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Specialist agents investigate all sorts of strange and weird happenings in their town by calling on their skills in mathematics and numeracy

programme.title: Danny & Mick
programme.desc: Danny is convinced it's Joy's birthday and he sets about organising a surprise party

programme.title: Our School
programme.desc: There is a trip to Christmas past in the magical city York, where the students experience life in the time of Charles Dickens. And there is an early present for Year 8 Ben, who is mad about trains.

programme.title: Our School
programme.desc: Everyone is nervous and excited for the all-singing, all-dancing end-of-term show. Children make the transition from primary to secondary school

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Haunted Holidays (2012)
programme.desc: The cowardly hound and his Mystery Machine companions find their winter holidays interrupted when a sinister living snowman begins to torment the owners of a local toy store. However, as Scooby and the gang investigate, they find themselves trapped within the shop's factory, with their frozen adversary lying in wait. Animated mystery adventure, with the voice of Matthew Lillard

programme.title: Dragons: Riders of Berk
programme.desc: Hiccup sets out on a dangerous treasure hunt when he thinks his father is disappointed in him

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors reveal why having different shaped teeth is essential to helping people eat food safely, and find out what it is like to be lifeguards in Operation Takeover

programme.title: Crackerjack!
programme.desc: Sam and Mark host a bumper edition of festive fun! Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt play an extra-messy game of Splatterjack and there is music from Becky Hill.

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger is excited that her UK family will be flying out to Australia for her birthday

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger reaches out to Ethan after his recent break-up

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger plans on getting her cousins to Australia by any means necessary. She decides to prank call them until they give up and get on that plane. Ginger's plan doesn't go well though and both Corey and Chris are unphased by her ‘Ginger Snaps'. Matt tries to get Robbie to admit that he's the reason the trip is cancelled, but Matt inadvertently tells Robbie his own secret: Matt is spending Christmas with Tabitha's family. Ethan is disappointed in Matt's efforts and vows to break Robbie on his own, but his plan backfires and Chris finds out Matt's skipping Christmas. Chris tells Matt that he's going to tell their Mum. Can Matt stop him?

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Robbie becomes increasingly frustrated with learning new instruments

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Chris finds out that his trap for Father Christmas won't work, Robbie gives up trying to learn new instruments and Ginger learns the real reason why no one is coming to Australia

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger works with Robbie and Heath to get info on Ethan

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger is worried that she has upset both Ethan and Robbie

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger and Ethan finally settle their argument and become pals again

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger celebrates her birthday on Christmas Eve. She has a wonderful day out with her father in Australia, but cannot help missing her UK family

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: In this episode we follow Rose as she celebrates Christmas with her family. Rose and her family visit the garden centre, where they find lots of festive decorations which they purchase alongside a Christmas tree. Once they arrive back at home, they all work together to decorate the house. After putting up the tree and leaving a sign for Santa in the window, Rose's parents take her for a magical experience: visiting Santa Claus in his grotto. Here, Rose tells Santa all about how well behaved she has been this year. Before leaving, Rose says goodbye to Santa and his reindeers, which are all outside in the snow. When Christmas Eve finally arrives, Rose attends a Christmas carol service with her family. Here they learn about the story of Jesus and sing some carols together. They head home to put on their festive pyjamas before going to bed. The next morning it's Christmas Day, and the family wake up to their presents under the tree.
