programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: In this festive special, Gray is looking forward to the Butterchurn Crescent Christmas lights display, which he hopes will brighten up the end of a difficult year - only for the event to be cancelled due to expense. Having landed a lucrative new contract at work, he takes it upon himself to save the lights - and Christmas

programme.title: Dolly Parton's Christmas Concert
programme.desc: Dolly Parton performs a Christmas concert featuring a host of her favourite festive songs

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. A compilation of the 2020 editions of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the 15th round of fixtures and look ahead to forthcoming contests

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts the game show, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda (2008)
programme.desc: A hero is needed to defend a peaceful valley community from attack by a rampaging snow leopard. The unlikely candidate for the job is a lazy panda, who enlists the aid of a legendary team of animal fighters and their wise master to teach him the martial arts skills he needs to fulfil his destiny. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jack Black, Dustin Hoffman, Angelina Jolie, Ian McShane and Jackie Chan

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda Holiday
programme.desc: Seasonal spin-off from the popular animated film. Po looks forward to helping his father prepare for the Winter Feast - until Shifu reveals this year he must take charge of hosting the celebrations himself

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 3 (2016)
programme.desc: The clumsy hero is reunited with his long-lost father, whose help he will need when a supernatural foe threatens all of China. Father and son travel to a secret village populated by other pandas, and must train them in martial arts so they can defend their home. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black, Bryan Cranston, Angelina Jolie and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Moana (2016)
programme.desc: To save her island home from a terrible curse, a brave Polynesian teenager must set sail and journey into the great blue yonder with the aid of a legendary hero. Disney animation, with the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Auli'i Cravalho, Rachel House and Temuera Morrison

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: Mackenzie Crook returns as the walking, talking scarecrow of Scatterbrook Farm. When he and his teenage friends Susan and John visit a scrapyard, Worzel hears the grumbling of a foul-mouthed old friend - carved ship's figurehead Saucy Nancy, who has been languishing there for years and longs to return to the sea. Worzel and the kids promise to get her there - but will her ship be waiting? Family adventure, with Shirley Henderson, Vanessa Redgrave, India Brown and Thierry Wickens

programme.title: The Wall Versus Celebrities
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts this special celebrity edition of the game show, which sees Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood teaming up with documentary-maker - and Strictly winner - Stacey Dooley to take on the Wall. Their aim is to win as much cash as possible for their chosen charities. But their chances rest on the unpredictability of the Wall and their unflinching trust in one another

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jo Brand, Joe Lycett, Joe Swash and Ruth Madeley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for this festive edition of the comedy game show. Did one of the panellists really program a satnav so their mother's trip to Lakeside ended up in the Lake District? And would one of the captains really hold up a train to get his daughter's cuddly toy back? Or were they just festive fibs? Rob Brydon hosts the fun

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Billy is speechless as Honey and Jay try to explain what he saw and storms out. Linda is desperate to get through to Mick and convince him to fight for her, but he fails to respond. However, when a distraught Tina comes looking for him, he finally snaps into action to help his sister. Ian realises just how horrible he has been and starts making amends - beginning with Peter and Bobby. But as the Albert Square residents gather to celebrate Christmas Eve, he finds himself targeted and terrified for his life yet again

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Another chance to see last year's festive edition of the comedy. The Shipmans travel to Wales to spend Christmas with the Wests, but for Gavin and Stacey, parenthood continues to present them with several challenges, and their 'baby' is now nine. Smithy also continues to commit to his fatherly role, making the journey to Barry at weekends and every second Wednesday to see 12-year-old Neil the Baby. Nessa has adopted her own style of mothering, while Pam, Mick and Gwen have become the epitome of doting grandparents. On Christmas Eve, Dawn makes a shocking discovery about Pete that jeopardises their marriage - again - while a visit to the pub becomes an eye-opener in more ways than one. Starring Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones

programme.title: Miranda's Games with Showbiz Names
programme.desc: Miranda Hart hosts this entertainment show in which three celebrity duos join her from the comfort of their own homes to compete in a series of riotous Christmas games. Those taking part are Shirley Ballas and actor boyfriend Danny Taylor, Robbie Williams and drummer Karl Brazil, and Jermaine Jenas and wife Ellie, while Shirley's fellow Strictly judge Craig Revel Horwood also puts in an appearance

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: This year marks Have I Got News for You's 30th anniversary. BBC One celebrates the past three decades with memories of the greatest moments, remembering the battles, the bust-ups, the guest hosts, seven prime ministers and five presidents - and not forgetting the tub of lard. Contributors include Paul Merton, Ian Hislop, Germaine Greer, Gary Lineker, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Ken Livingstone and Alexander Armstrong

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ronnie Barker's final screen appearance, originally aired two months after his death in 2005, in which he and Ronnie Corbett choose their favourite sketches, songs and monologues from their festive shows, including the Milkman's Christmas Message, Christmas Day in the Yukon and an interpretation of Alice Through the Looking Glass. Katie Melua sings Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, before the duo sign off for the very last time

programme.title: Midnight Mass - BBC
programme.desc: Live service from the Roman Catholic cathedral of St Peter and St Paul in Bristol. The service is led by the Bishop of Clifton, the Rt Rev Declan Lang, and the preacher is Canon Bosco MacDonald. The Cathedral's Schola Cantorum, conducted by Richard Jeffrey-Gray, sing Christmas favourites including Silent Night and Hark! The Herald Angels Sing

programme.title: I Give It a Year (2013)
programme.desc: A couple marry after a whirlwind romance, but family and friends have doubts about the relationship. As their first anniversary approaches, they both begin to wonder if they are right for each other, especially when faced with more appealing alternative partners. Romantic comedy, with Rose Byrne, Rafe Spall, Anna Faris and Simon Baker

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Gruffalo
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book. It tells the tale of a little mouse who takes a walk through the woods in search of a nut, only to encounter a fox, an owl and a snake who all want to eat him. To escape their clutches, the quick-witted rodent creates an imaginary monster to scare away his attackers - but it turns out to be all too real. Robbie Coltrane heads the voice cast as the Gruffalo, with James Corden as the Mouse and Rob Brydon, John Hurt and Tom Wilkinson as the predators

programme.title: The Gruffalo's Child
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the sequel to Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book, reuniting all the voice cast from the original. The Gruffalo has raised his daughter on tales of the big, bad mouse, the only creature in the forest he is frightened of. Despite her father's warnings, she sets off in search of this scary creature, along the way encountering the snake, owl and the fox. But when she still has no luck finding the evil rodent, she begins to think he does not exist after all. Robbie Coltrane, James Corden and Rob Brydon lend their voices, with Shirley Henderson as Gruffalo Jr

programme.title: Lou (2017)
programme.desc: Oscar-nominated Pixar animation about a pile of lost and found items coming together to teach a toy-stealing schoolyard bully a valuable lesson

programme.title: Christmas Day Eucharist
programme.desc: Senior Pastor, Bishop Paul McCalla, and Peter Sutcliffe, Senior Pastor at the Nechells Outreach Centre lead a festive act of worship, live from the Church of God of Prophecy in Birmingham. The gospel-themed affair features a small group of singers and musicians, all socially distanced in compliance with Covid regulations. Music includes some of the best-loved carols, including Joy to the World, Hark! The Herald Angels Sing and Mary Did You Know?

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Keen but accident-prone dragon Zog gets himself into mischief while learning how to fly, roar, breathe fire and fight knights in his first four years at Dragon School. Each year he meets a kindly young girl who patches up his bumps and bruises, but can she help him with his trickiest challenge yet? It's year five and he has to capture a princess. Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's book, featuring the voices of Lenny Henry, Tracey Ullman, Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Kit Harington

programme.title: Top of the Pops Christmas Special
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo review the hottest stars and biggest songs of the last 12 months from a very different year in music. Featuring performances from AJ Tracey and Aitch, Becky Hill, Celeste, Clean Bandit and Mabel, Ella Henderson, Jamie Cullum, Joel Corry and MNEK, and KSI featuring Craig David and Digital Farm Animals. Plus, the Christmas number one is revealed

programme.title: Inner Workings (2016)
programme.desc: Award-winning Animated Disney short that brings to life the constant internal struggle between head and heart. Paul works in an office at Boring, Boring and Glum. Inside him, his brain struggles to motivate him to work and battles against his heart's desire to break out of the dull routine and have some fun instead

programme.title: Early Man (2018)
programme.desc: Young Dug's sheltered life with his Stone Age tribe is shattered by bronze-mining invaders led by greedy Lord Nooth. To win his home back, desperate Dug gambles on challenging these more sophisticated folk in a contest they love, but he only knows from rock paintings - football. Director Nick Park's animated adventure, with the voices of Eddie Redmayne, Timothy Spall, Rob Brydon, Miriam Margolyes and Johnny Vegas

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's second children's book about the accident-prone dragon. Zog is helping his friend Princess Pearl to provide much-needed medical care to all manner of creatures, assisted by knight Sir Gadabout. However, when a storm forces them to return to Pearl's home at the palace, her uncle the king does not approve of her career - at least until he falls ill himself. Narrated by Lenny Henry, with the voices of Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty's annual address to the nation and the people of the Commonwealth

programme.title: Coco (2017)
programme.desc: A Mexican boy longs to be a guitarist, despite his family hating music. During the Day of the Dead festivities, he gets transported to the land of the dead, and with the help of a cunning skeleton, hopes to find his ancestor and learn the secrets of his family's history. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Anthony Gonzalez and Gael Garcia Bernal

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A chance to see the 25 most memorable dances of all time, voted for by the public, and a brand-new Christmas-themed group number. Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman are also joined by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood, the show's regular professionals and some very special celebrity guests to take a look back at some of the most iconic moments ever to grace the Strictly ballroom. From crowd favourites to perfect 40s and trophy-winning performances, this show has everything necessary to add a little Strictly sparkle to Christmas Day

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a special celebrity edition of the game show, with contestants Holly Willoughby, Alan Carr and Oti Mabuse competing to win a cash prize for the charity of their choice. Helping the contestants to win as much money as possible will be a panel of seven celebrity experts, each with their own Christmas specialist subject. Alexander Armstrong, Christopher Biggins, Clare Balding, Howard Donald, the Rev Kate Bottley, Olly Smith and Ainsley Harriott will help to answer questions on topics ranging from Christmas dinner to panto

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh is in charge as the popular game show returns for a one-off festive special. Members of a panel comprising comedian Jimmy Carr, pop star Danny Jones, actress Emilia Fox, boxer Amir Khan, comedian Sue Perkins and presenter Anita Rani do their best to fill in the blanks to help the contestants win a selection of prizes - or at the very least go home with the famous Blankety Blank chequebook and pen - before the winner goes through to the supermatch game to play for the jackpot

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: It's December 1965, and everyone at Nonnatus House is looking forward to the festive celebrations, but nothing seems to go quite to plan. Sister Monica Joan is rushed to hospital, and Trixie is incensed that her dress allowance has been replaced with a marriage bureau consultation. Elsewhere, the circus arrives in Poplar, leading to an exciting adventure for Nurse Phyllis Crane. Seasonal instalment of the hit drama, starring Jenny Agutter, Linda Bassett, Judy Parfitt, Helen George, Peter Davison and featuring the voice of Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian identifies his would-be killer and the police are quick to make an arrest. But have they got the right person? Denise comes face to face with a ghost from the past, and Stacey is surprised to receive an expensive gift from Martin. Mick spends an awkward Christmas lunch with Tina and Shirley at Gray's house

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Brendan O'Carroll's indomitable matriarch returns for the first of two festive specials, which finds the family adjusting to the new normal. Buster and Dermot have got new jobs raising awareness of the dangers of coronavirus, while a flasher is terrorising the neighbourhood. Winnie and Agnes are more concerned with winning the chance to perform an alternative Queen's speech - and may be able to provide Father Damien with some tips on getting his message across

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The villagers celebrate Christmas, and vicar Geraldine learns there is a heavy price to be paid for her kindness when she agrees to attend three slap-up lunches and takes part in a Brussels sprout-eating competition. Festive special from 1996, guest starring Peter Capaldi and Mel Giedroyc

programme.title: On Christmas Night
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo narrates the story of the first Christmas from Luke's Gospel, when angels appeared to shepherds to proclaim the news of Jesus's birth

programme.title: Paul McCartney at The Cavern Club
programme.desc: The former Beatle's barnstorming 2018 performance at Liverpool's Cavern Club, where his career with the band started out. Around 250 people crammed into the sweaty venue to hear the 28-song set featuring classics he performed when the Beatles started out in the early 1960s, plus songs from his then-new solo album, Egypt Station

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Foreign Affairs Committee's session on Xinjiang detention camps with evidence from Dolkun Isa, President, World Uyghur Congress and Schona Jolly QC, Chair, Bar Human Rights Committee and then from Nury Turkel, Commissioner, United States Commission on International Religious Freedom and Professor Fionnuala Ní Aoláin, from Tuesday 15 December

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, on the Covid-19 pandemic and the possible need to strengthen the top level of Covid-19 restrictions in Scotland as the whole of the Scottish mainland moves to level 4 from Boxing Day, from Tuesday 22 December

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy select committee session on the impact of border delays on UK business and security of supply, from earlier today

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including the members' business debate on the impact of the coronavirus on loneliness and social isolation followed by a ministerial statement on the Covid-19 vaccine and testing programmes.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Scottish Parliament to the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon.

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Live coverage of proceedings in the Scottish Parliament in Holyrood including portfolio questions to Scottish government ministers covering education and skills, health and sport, and communities and local government followed by a ministerial statement on Brexit and Stage 3 proceedings on the Scottish General Election (Coronavirus) Bill.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Scottish Parliament to the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon.

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of proceedings in the Scottish Parliament in Holyrood including portfolio questions to Scottish government ministers covering education and skills, health and sport, and communities and local government followed by a ministerial statement on Brexit and Stage 3 proceedings on the Scottish General Election (Coronavirus) Bill, from earlier today

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Downing Street Coronavirus News Conference
programme.desc: The news conference held in Downing Street by the Prime Minister, Boris Johnson, on the announcement of a Tier 4 for London and many areas of the South East as well as changes to the restrictions over Christmas, alongside the government's chief scientific adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance, and the Chief Medical Officer for England, Professor Chris Whitty, from Saturday 19 December

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer giving a speech on devolution across the UK and moving power out of London, from Monday 21 December

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, Tristram Hunt, delivering this year's Orwell Memorial Lecture on 'Decolonising The Wonder House: Orwell, Empire and the Museum', from Monday 16 November.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to the Executive Office in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Political Highlights
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of political and parliamentary highlights

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including backbench business debates on the regulation and prevention of online harms and on International Men's Day followed by an adjournment debate on the Department for Health and Social Care's Independent Reconfiguration Panel.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Affairs select committee session on cross-border cooperation on policing, security and criminal justice with evidence from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, the National Crime Agency and Her Majesty's Customs and Revenue, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the International Trade Secretary Liz Truss and her team of ministers.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of debates on issues affecting Scotland

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology committee and the Health and Social Care committee looking at the performance of NHS Test and Trace with evidence from the Executive Chair, Dido Harding, and its Chief Medical Adviser, Dr Susan Hopkins.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the prospect for reconciliation for Northern Ireland's two communities - Unionist and Nationalist? Stephen Sackur speaks to Billy Hutchinson, advocate of co-existence

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.desc: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, Tristram Hunt, delivering this year's Orwell Memorial Lecture on 'Decolonising The Wonder House: Orwell, Empire and the Museum', from Monday 16 November.

How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. programme.desc: Donnie Mackay investigates flooding along the River Tay. Lorry driver Murdo John Mackay crosses Scotland in all weathers, and it's CalMac handyman Alasdair MacIver's last day at work after 28 years at sea. In South Uist, the SSEN team members make a special delivery.

programme.desc: The remarkable story of Millie and Syd, two children from Hertfordshire who ran away to their grandparents' in 1949

programme.title: Bho Chaorain Gu Consoles
programme.desc: A nostalgic trip down memory lane, remembering the presents that brought magic to cold Christmas mornings over the decades. There are all kinds of gifts - puzzles and games have come and gone over the years, from the head-scratching angst of the Rubiks Cube through to the family fun of Monopoly, while electronic games - from Space Invaders all the way through to Playstations - are abidingly popular

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Hibernian v St Mirren. A chance to see the top-flight encounter at Easter Road. Commentary from Hugh Dan MacLennan

programme.title: An t-Inneal Espresso/The Espresso Machine
programme.desc: Drama revolving around a high-end espresso machine. Ealasaid loaned her machine to Ailig's girlfriend Una, and desperately wants it back. Unfortunately, Ailig and Una just broke up

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Bioran (Stick Man)
programme.desc: The adventures of the father of a family of sticks, who crosses great distances to try to get home in time for Christmas. Stick Man lives in the family tree with his Stick Lady Love and their stick children three. However, it's dangerous being a Stick Man - a dog wants to play with him, a swan builds her nest with him and he even ends up on a fire

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Keen but accident-prone dragon Zog gets himself into mischief while learning how to fly, roar, breathe fire and fight knights in his first four years at Dragon School. Each year he meets a kindly young girl who patches up his bumps and bruises, but can she help him with his trickiest challenge yet? It's year five and he has to capture a princess. Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's book, featuring the voices of Lenny Henry, Tracey Ullman, Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Kit Harington

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Robert Robertson reads Tormod an t-Seilcheag a Shàbhail an Nollaig, written and illustrated by Sue Hendra and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald

programme.title: Saoghal Bodach na Nollaig
programme.desc: Tony and Vito get up to mischief

programme.title: Cinema Gadelica
programme.desc: How film-makers have been drawn to the Highlands and Islands to shoot amid the spectacular scenery of the Gaidhealtachd. Discover James Bond’s Glencoe birthplace, as revealed in Skyfall. Join Harry Potter on his way to Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry via the Glenfinnan Viaduct, and set out on Ridley Scott’s Prometheus to discover the origins of humankind – starting the mission at the Quiraing in Skye

programme.desc: A special guest star ruins the Mod and it is Christmas in the lighthouse

programme.title: Alleluia na Nollaige/Christmas Alleluia

programme.desc: During the winter holidays, six-year-old girl Pigtail wants to learn skating, but, shortly before Christmas, her baby brother appears to the world and turns everything upside down

programme.title: Zog agus na Dotairean Sgiathach/Zog and The Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Animated comedy about keen, young dragon Zog and his friends Princess Pearl and the brave Knight Gadabout. Zog, Pearl and Gadabout are now a flying doctor trio, caring for creatures including a mermaid, a unicorn and a lion. However, when bad weather forces them to land at the palace garden, Pearl is locked up by the King. Zog and Gadabout try in vain to set her free

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis reads An Nollaig aig Mog, written and illustrated by Judith Kerr and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Sol ()
programme.desc: Sol, a 10-year old boy who is grieving after the loss of his grandmother Nonee, tries to figure out a way forward without her

programme.title: Lena - An Croitear Og/Lena - The Young Crofter
programme.desc: The life of 10-year-old Lena MacKenzie, from the Isle of Harris, who wants to continue the traditional agricultural way of life that she is being brought up with. The future of the family croft is in safe hands with this passionate young crofter who likes to be at the heart of any crofting work her father has to do

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Mòd 2020
programme.desc: Highlights from previous Children's Mod, focusing on solo singing, traditional singing, folk groups, choral singing and winners of the An Comunn Gàidhealach Silver Medals

programme.title: Cearcaill Ceo
programme.desc: The Lewis car rally culture of the 70s and 80s, which saw a young generation with keen engineering instincts watching as TV brought rallying into their homes for the first time

programme.title: Tide Lines 2020
programme.desc: Music show featuring the band Tide Lines in a specially recorded performance in Glasgow’s SWG3. With the absence of any gigs in 2020, Tide Lines took the opportunity to showcase new performances of classic material and some brand new material in a specially recorded session. This programme features a selection from that performance originally aired in July 2020. Alongside fans' favourites, the band perform a new Gaelic song and also a beautiful collaboration with Tamzene

programme.title: Charley Pride
programme.desc: The improbable journey of the trailblazing country music superstar, from his humble beginnings as a sharecropper’s son on a cotton farm in segregated Sledge, Mississippi, to his career as a baseball player and his meteoric rise in music. This new documentary reveals how Pride’s love for music led him from the Delta to a larger, grander world. In the 1940s, radio transcended racial barriers, making it possible for Pride to grow up listening to and imitating Grand Ole Opry stars like Ernest Tubb and Roy Acuff. At a time when African-American singers were more notable for R&B hits, Charley Pride followed his passion for country music, overcoming obstacles through determination and raw talent to make a lasting impact on the genre and create a legacy that continues today

programme.title: Air Iomall-Music from the Edge
programme.desc: Two of Scotland's leading young traditional musicians, Charlie Grey and Joseph Peach, take a musical voyage on the Dutch vessel Wylde Swan to some of the most remote islands off Scotland's north-west coast

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with contestants Martine McCutcheon and her husband, Joel Dommett and Denise Van Outen, and Aston Merrygold and JB Gill from JLS racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: In this edition, Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from Liverpool, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the big finale game

programme.title: Fred Claus (2007)
programme.desc: Santa's selfish brother Fred has been ostracised by his family for years, but when his crooked ways land him in jail, his brother comes along to bail him out on one condition - he must return to the family business and make toys for Christmas. But instead of cleaning up his act, Fred pushes Santa's patience to the limit with his far from festive attitude. Comedy, starring Vince Vaughn, Paul Giamatti, Rachel Weisz, Miranda Richardson and Kevin Spacey

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Memorable moments from the show with David Dickinson and his team of antiques dealers. Karen Dalmeny is brought a bowl that may or may not be genuine Worcester. Does she know the answer? Michael Hogben views a collection of vintage puppets, Jon O'Marah tries to buy an old sword and an Amethyst pendant brings tears at auction

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Celebrity version of the quiz show hosted by Warwick Davis, in which a team of five Olympians answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge for the chance to win £125,000 for charity

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts a special seasonal edition of the quiz, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine in the hope of winning £20,000 for charity. This time, the famous faces taking part are comedian Joe Pasquale, TV personality Rustie Lee, and actress Nina Wadia

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a festive episode in which the Rev Kate Bottley, Dr Ranj Singh, Made in Chelsea's Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo and comedian Ed Byrne pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mack covers his tracks, Liv reveals a secret, and Paddy reels

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The police call at the salon flat and haul Gary in for questioning regarding the disappearance of Rick Neelan. Leanne makes an effort for Nick and Sam, and Chesney and Kirk experience a market downturn

programme.title: Chefs' Burnt Bits
programme.desc: In a special selection of comical bloopers, famous TV cooks including Gino D'Acampo, James Martin, Ainsley Harriott and Lisa Faulkner look back at times they made mistakes live on air for millions of viewers to see

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah calls at the salon flat and rails at Gary, accusing him of moving Rick's body to save his own skin, which Maria overhears. Leanne persuades Nick to put his son first, and Roy and Nina urge Shona to talk to David

programme.title: Jennifer Saunders' Memory Lane
programme.desc: Jennifer slips behind the wheel of a classic red Jaguar E-Type, joined in the passenger seat by Hollywood actor Michael Sheen whom she quizzes about his life and times. Heading to Port Talbot where Michael grew up, they stop at key locations to prompt personal stories from his childhood right up to the present day. Drawing on evocative family photos and colourful local archive, Michael's recollections are by turns funny, surprising and incredibly poignant

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Knocked Up (2007)
programme.desc: Ambitious journalist Alison's one-night stand with directionless layabout Ben has unexpected consequences when she discovers she is pregnant. However, the unlikely parents-to-be decide to give the relationship a try for their baby's sake, forcing Ben to face the challenge of accepting responsibility for the first time in his life. Romantic comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Katherine Heigl and Paul Rudd

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Vegetarian food ideas, presented by Henry Firth and Ian Theasby. First on the menu are Sunday roast, vegan cheese in a toasted sandwich, sticky toffee pudding and snack ideas using avocados. The hosts are joined by actress Sadie Frost, who tries their jalfrezi curry

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news, current affairs and entertainment, with guests including actors Cliff Parisi and Annabelle Apsion, revealing what viewers can look forward to in tomorrow night's festive episode of Call the Midwife

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Santa Claus: The Movie (1985)
programme.desc: An impulsive elf walks out on Father Christmas during the hectic preparations for the festive season, and heads for New York determined to make a name for himself. However, he falls into the clutches of an evil toy manufacturer, who has a devilish money-making plan up his sleeve. Family fantasy adventure, starring Dudley Moore, John Lithgow, David Huddleston, Burgess Meredith and Judy Cornwell

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a one-off special going behind the scenes of TV's most surreal singing show. The programme features previously unseen interviews and highlights of the series that turned the nation into couch detectives at the start of 2020. Disguised in extravagant costumes including a duck, hedgehog and octopus, 12 celebrities took to the stage and competed to stay anonymous. Set with the task of identifying the Masked Singers were the panel of celebrity detectives - Ken Jeong, Davina McCall, Rita Ora and Jonathan Ross

programme.title: Katherine Jenkins: Christmas Spectacular from the Royal Albert Hall
programme.desc: The mezzo-soprano presents a concert of seasonal favourites, captured live in London, with guests including bass-baritone Bryn Terfel, Italian tenor Alberto Urso, American Broadway star Marisha Wallace, English National Ballet lead principal Erina Takahashi, the Royal Air Force Regiment Band and actors Vanessa Redgrave and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which broadcaster Clare Balding, actor and presenter Joe Swash and actress Sherrie Hewson answer questions to win turns on a large arcade-style 'coin-pusher' machine. The more counters they collect, the greater the prize fund, with the last one standing playing for a £20,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Dancer Jordan Banjo, actress Helen George and former football manager Harry Redknapp line up to 'say what they see' as they try to guess the familiar phrases depicted by festive-themed animated clues and win thousands of pounds for their chosen charities. Stephen Mulhern hosts

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Priya wants Al to herself, Charity is left angry and hurt, and Cain organises a romantic night-in

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly tearfully explains to Maria that she needs to see Gary as he's the only person in contact with her dad. Toyah and Imran are asked to foster a baby, and David and Shona prepare for a final Christmas together

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian revives another classic game show with a special festive edition of The Price Is Right. Eight members of the studio audience will be invited to 'Come on Down' and spin the famous wheel, guess the value of assorted items, and compete in the Epic Showcase for the chance to win a mountain of Christmas prizes

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Christmas is just around the corner in Chigwell, and there are big changes in the women's lives. Dorien is now ruling the roost, having bought the house, and she and Tracey have been getting on each other's nerves. To make matters worse, Tracey is missing sister Sharon, who is stuck on a cruise ship in Costa Rica, and Dorien is up to her old tricks with a rich dentist. Does he know what he's getting himself into? Return of the comedy, starring Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with Les Dennis and Ami Metcalf

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan presents an hour of festive fun and chat, joined by comedian and presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney, soon to be seen in another series of their thrill-seeking travelogue Breaking Dad, and Olympic star Mo Farah, most recently seen battling the bugs on I'm a Celebrity. His other guests are actress Michelle Keegan, popular stand-up Kevin Bridges, comic and Celebrity Juice star Emily Atack and chart-topping singer Jess Glynne, who also performs in the studio

programme.title: Carols at Christmas 2020
programme.desc: Michael Ball and Alfie Boe host a traditional Christmas Eve celebration from St Elizabeth's Church in Greater Manchester. The duo lead the musical performances, which also feature acclaimed jazz singer Jamie Cullum, singer-songwriter Ella Henderson, the Kingdom Choir and West End star Marisha Wallace

programme.title: Chefs' Burnt Bits
programme.desc: In a special selection of comical bloopers, famous TV cooks including Gino D'Acampo, James Martin, Ainsley Harriott and Lisa Faulkner look back at times they made mistakes live on air for millions of viewers to see

programme.title: Casino Royale (1967)
programme.desc: Ageing spy James Bond comes out of retirement to tackle a mysterious mastermind who wants to take over the world. Meanwhile, another agent disguises himself as Bond to face the evil genius over the baccarat table. Spoof spy adventure, starring David Niven, Peter Sellers, Woody Allen, Orson Welles, Deborah Kerr and Ursula Andress

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites celebrities to take part in a special edition of the quiz show, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Make It Pop
programme.desc: The kids get snowed in at MacKendrick and learn the real meaning of Christmas. They also meet Linc, a mysterious new student

programme.title: Wishfart
programme.desc: The city is overtaken by terrifying and delicious hordes of gingerdead cookies. As the trio take shelter, they realise that they must find the cause of the disaster

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean looks back over his life, recalling how he acquired his beloved teddy bear and learned to drive. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean's electricity is cut off when Mrs Wicket sees her electricity bill, but he has a back-up idea - a wind turbine. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: It's cold, the boiler is broken, and Mr Bean has to get warm - fast. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Summer rears its hot, cheeky head in Grimsby, but Dave has found a way to keep the Spud flat nice and cool

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Bubbles, Blossom and Buttercup accidentally break their Christmas gift for the Professor, and must fight against time and evil villains to get him a new one before the big day

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Piers Morgan and Susanna Reid relive the moments created by the real stars of 2020 - celebrating Covid heroes up and down the country, from the likes of Captain Sir Tom to the staff of the NHS who saved so many lives

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly is joined by special guests including Kylie Minogue, Michael Ball, Alfie Boe and Chris Kamara as she lets viewers into the secrets of her festive period and shares joy and laughter. Plus, her family of regular experts provide fashion and beauty inspiration

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: For the first time ever, Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host a special episode of the programme for Christmas Day. They offer heart-warming surprises and are joined by special guests and some familiar faces as they celebrate the most iconic day of the year

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: For the first time ever on Christmas Day, James invites viewers around to his house and kicks off the day's celebrations in style, serving up a feast of festive food and entertainment for guests Denise Van Outen and Chris Evans. He is joined in the kitchen by The Ritz's head chef John Williams, and Bake Off Creme De La Creme winner Mark Tilling treats viewers to some of his deliciously decadent chocolate creations

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Christopher Biggins, Martine McCutcheon and Brian Turner for this Christmas special. On the menu are festive bubble patties, Thai turkey and coconut broth with chilli, coconut and coriander scones, and chocolate bread and butter pudding with spiced cherries. Plus, drinks expert Olly Smith is on hand to knock up some festive tipples, including an alcoholic chocolate milkshake

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty's annual address to the nation and the people of the Commonwealth

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers in full HD, using the latest technology. A postman is attacked by a turkey, a toddler asks Santa for beer and a runaway car wrecks a grotto

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts with a special festive episode of the popular general knowledge quiz. A team of five panto performers takes on the Tenable Tower and answers a series of taxing top tens. Can its members bag themselves the ultimate Christmas present and walk away with a £125,000 jackpot?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Colin Jackson, Anne Diamond, Josie Long and Nicky Campbell answer general knowledge questions and work as a team to take on all five of the ruthless Chasers and secure a charity prize. Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace have donned their glam rags for the show, but will the celebs outshine the Chasers? Bradley Walsh presents

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is full of resentment, Al's secret is revealed, and it remains to be seen whether the Dingle wedding will go ahead

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After David and Shona admit that they still love each other and want to make a go of it, he forms a plan. Toyah and Imran adjust to becoming parents overnight, and Leanne hides away in her flat, consumed with grief

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec welcome the most memorable and talented acts from the past 14 years, inviting them to collaborate and perform together for the ultimate Christmas extravaganza. Joining the hosts are judges Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo, who will be ready to be impressed by a whole host of talent. All four will also be stepping out from their seats and into the limelight for the very first time in what promises to be a festive-filled show

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: The acerbic host passes judgement on what the great British public watched in 2020 - and didn't - and highlights some fascinating foreign TV shows that managed to make it to air. Shows under his gaze include gripping dramas White House Farm, Quiz and Des, as well as the latest offerings from the streaming services including dating shows such as Love Is Blind and property shows like Selling Sunset

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams unwraps a batch of brand-new slip-ups for Christmas and reviews the greatest TV blunders of 2020, featuring some of the UK's most-famous faces. David reveals which world leaders and members of the royal family have all been caught out on camera

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.desc: The comedian revives another classic game show with a special festive edition of The Price Is Right. Eight members of the studio audience will be invited to 'Come on Down' and spin the famous wheel, guess the value of assorted items, and compete in the Epic Showcase for the chance to win a mountain of Christmas prizes

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the quiz show in which teams from ITV's biggest soaps battle it out. Jack P Shepherd captains the Coronation Street team and is joined by Simon Gregson and Sair Khan, while Mark Charnock takes the lead for Emmerdale, alongside Karen Blick and Jay Kontzle

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan presents an hour of festive fun and chat, joined by comedian and presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney, soon to be seen in another series of their thrill-seeking travelogue Breaking Dad, and Olympic star Mo Farah, most recently seen battling the bugs on I'm a Celebrity. programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha is joined by chef Eran Tibi to dish up a family feast from the Middle East. The menu comprises an Israeli mezze of stuffed brick and sweet potato hummus for breakfast, mussakhan for lunch, Eran's aubergine, tomato and beef stew for dinner and Nadia's cigar baklava with cheat's pistachio ice cream for dessert

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean's electricity is cut off when Mrs Wicket sees her electricity bill, but he has a back-up idea - a wind turbine. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: It's cold, the boiler is broken, and Mr Bean has to get warm - fast. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Summer rears its hot, cheeky head in Grimsby, but Dave has found a way to keep the Spud flat nice and cool

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Bubbles, Blossom and Buttercup accidentally break their Christmas gift for the Professor, and must fight against time and evil villains to get him a new one before the big day

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Piers Morgan and Susanna Reid relive the moments created by the real stars of 2020 - celebrating Covid heroes up and down the country, from the likes of Captain Sir Tom to the staff of the NHS who saved so many lives

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly is joined by special guests including Kylie Minogue, Michael Ball, Alfie Boe and Chris Kamara as she lets viewers into the secrets of her festive period and shares joy and laughter. Plus, her family of regular experts provide fashion and beauty inspiration

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: For the first time ever, Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host a special episode of the programme for Christmas Day. They offer heart-warming surprises and are joined by special guests and some familiar faces as they celebrate the most iconic day of the year

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: For the first time ever on Christmas Day, James invites viewers around to his house and kicks off the day's celebrations in style, serving up a feast of festive food and entertainment for guests Denise Van Outen and Chris Evans. He is joined in the kitchen by The Ritz's head chef John Williams, and Bake Off Creme De La Creme winner Mark Tilling treats viewers to some of his deliciously decadent chocolate creations

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Christopher Biggins, Martine McCutcheon and Brian Turner for this Christmas special. On the menu are festive bubble patties, Thai turkey and coconut broth with chilli, coconut and coriander scones, and chocolate bread and butter pudding with spiced cherries. Plus, drinks expert Olly Smith is on hand to knock up some festive tipples, including an alcoholic chocolate milkshake

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty's annual address to the nation and the people of the Commonwealth

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers in full HD, using the latest technology. A postman is attacked by a turkey, a toddler asks Santa for beer and a runaway car wrecks a grotto

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts with a special festive episode of the popular general knowledge quiz. A team of five panto performers takes on the Tenable Tower and answers a series of taxing top tens. Can its members bag themselves the ultimate Christmas present and walk away with a £125,000 jackpot?

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Colin Jackson, Anne Diamond, Josie Long and Nicky Campbell answer general knowledge questions and work as a team to take on all five of the ruthless Chasers and secure a charity prize. Anne Hegerty, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace have donned their glam rags for the show, but will the celebs outshine the Chasers? Bradley Walsh presents

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is full of resentment, Al's secret is revealed, and it remains to be seen whether the Dingle wedding will go ahead

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After David and Shona admit that they still love each other and want to make a go of it, he forms a plan. Toyah and Imran adjust to becoming parents overnight, and Leanne hides away in her flat, consumed with grief

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec welcome the most memorable and talented acts from the past 14 years, inviting them to collaborate and perform together for the ultimate Christmas extravaganza. Joining the hosts are judges Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo, who will be ready to be impressed by a whole host of talent. All four will also be stepping out from their seats and into the limelight for the very first time in what promises to be a festive-filled show

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: The acerbic host passes judgement on what the great British public watched in 2020 - and didn't - and highlights some fascinating foreign TV shows that managed to make it to air. Shows under his gaze include gripping dramas White House Farm, Quiz and Des, as well as the latest offerings from the streaming services including dating shows such as Love Is Blind and property shows like Selling Sunset

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams unwraps a batch of brand-new slip-ups for Christmas and reviews the greatest TV blunders of 2020, featuring some of the UK's most-famous faces. David reveals which world leaders and members of the royal family have all been caught out on camera

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian revives another classic game show with a special festive edition of The Price Is Right. Eight members of the studio audience will be invited to 'Come on Down' and spin the famous wheel, guess the value of assorted items, and compete in the Epic Showcase for the chance to win a mountain of Christmas prizes

programme.title: The Big Quiz
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the quiz show in which teams from ITV's biggest soaps battle it out. Jack P Shepherd captains the Coronation Street team and is joined by Simon Gregson and Sair Khan, while Mark Charnock takes the lead for Emmerdale, alongside Karen Blick and Jay Kontzle

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan presents an hour of festive fun and chat, joined by comedian and presenter Bradley Walsh and his son Barney, soon to be seen in another series of their thrill-seeking travelogue Breaking Dad, and Olympic star Mo Farah, most recently seen battling the bugs on I'm a Celebrity. His other guests are actress Michelle Keegan, popular stand-up Kevin Bridges, comic and Celebrity Juice star Emily Atack and chart-topping singer Jess Glynne, who also performs in the studio

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: All Lois wants is help with the Christmas shopping, but when the family fail her she walks out on them, leaving the remaining Griffins to try to save the holiday season

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: As the Griffins prepare for the festive season by decorating their tree, Meg finds an ornament of the manger and Peter tells the story of Christmas - with a twist. His account begins with Joseph and his friend Robby spotting Mary in their village and becoming acquainted. After many dates, the future Holy guardian is annoyed by his girlfriend's reluctance to have sex, and she claims there is something special intended for her

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie finally says his first word, but it is a rude one, leading to Lois being shunned by the other mums and going on a mission to find out where he learnt it

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie breaks up with Rupert after discovering he used to belong to Chris and tries to get revenge by getting a new teddy bear

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger converts to Judaism after he feels unappreciated by the family. In a twist of fate, he puts on Santa's suit and becomes 'Schmanta'

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Hayley visits a hypnotherapy doctor, who is really Roger in another of his alter-egos, and ends up regressing back to her six-year-old self. Meanwhile, Steve and his friends buy a slow cooker and try to make the perfect pork

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine gets a job at Stan's office, which leaves him unable to flirt with his co-workers - and to make matters even worse, jealousy leads to an awful encounter

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts this festive edition of the comedy quiz show where celebrities cold-call real people around the UK. Tonight, Baga Chipz, AJ and Curtis Pritchard, Tom Lucy, Seann Walsh and Samira Mighty try to win some normal people £2,500 and some teeth whitening

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden, still pretending to be regular guy Sam in Kate's company, lands a job selling Christmas trees in a festive edition of the comedy. Meanwhile, Mark Zuckerberg takes an interest in Walden's business, and Berta lifts Alan's spirits

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons try to impress dental nurse Cece in Cardiff, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes she liked best

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve offers his services to Mike. Judy and Gary await the results of their fertility tests. The Duckworths receive a shock from the VAT man. Fred invites Maureen to the ballet

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths fear they will be made bankrupt. Steve tries to find work with his old contacts. Kevin is annoyed when Bill asks him if he is having an affair with Natalie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly starts work at the vets, Tara plans Kim's hen night, while Steve asks Alan to host his stag party at the Woolpack. Chris and Zoe get a shock when they visit Steve's office

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe and Chris try to work out why Steve is conning Kim. Paddy's attitude towards Kelly causes Zoe concern. Kim tells Steve that she has never been happier

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When armed robbers hold up the night mail train, Joe is forced to work with old adversary DI Ludlow (David Calder) to catch the perpetrators. However, Ludlow's unwillingness to include the local police in the investigation leads Joe to believe he may be hiding something. Elsewhere, Peggy is excited to learn she has been left a legacy by a relation of her late husband. Eric Sykes makes a guest appearance

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally considers a job which will take her away from the family home. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Rog gets busy organising the team's first match, while Mim tries to cheer up Bob after Ginnie gives him his marching orders. Maggie's sister inadvertently lets slip a secret - with devastating consequences. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Mutiny on the Buses (1972)
programme.desc: Chirpy bus driver Stan Butler lands himself in trouble as he struggles to raise enough money to marry his girlfriend, and on top of training his unemployed brother-in-law for a job at the depot he has to contend with an officious new manager. Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A trio of Australian sheep-shearers arrive in Aidensfield to help out at Grimedale Farm, but a missing watch puts one of them behind bars. Rosie is surprised by a face from the past and has to choose between two tempting offers for the future. Elsewhere, Peggy plans to start a laundry business, but leaves David doing all the work, and Phil and Gina make a life-changing decision. Former Neighbours actors Blair McDonough and Martin Crewes guest star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve starts to panic about his business, and the lads attempt to drag him off on his stag night. Tara assures Viv she has planned something memorable for Kim's hen night

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is confident that he has saved his business and prepares for the wedding. Butch steals a horse from Home Farm. Sal finds out what Steve has done

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie worries that when Sally returns Kevin will drop her. Ken discovers his redundancy money is not as much as he expected. Jack desperately tries to borrow some money

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: Quiz
programme.desc: Persecuted and hounded by the public and press, Charles, Diana and Tecwen head to court. At first it seems the case for the prosecution is solid, but as the case for the defence gets underway, the jury learn there is far more to the story than they first thought. Drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen and Sian Clifford

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective is ordered to take a holiday for the benefit of his health, and with Hastings and Miss Lemon in tow, reluctantly heads for the tranquillity of Devon's coast, where his relaxation is soon ruined by the discovery of a socialite's murdered body on the beach. Several people had reason for wishing Arlena Stuart dead, but high on the list of suspects are her husband, teenage son and a troubled vicar. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser, Philip Jackson and Pauline Moran star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?

programme.title: Holiday on the Buses (1973)
programme.desc: Bus driver Stan and conductor Jack are fired and wind up working at a holiday camp in Wales, where they fall foul of the security officer - none other than their old adversary Inspector Blake, who has also lost his job and been forced into alternative employment. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Anna Karen and Doris Hare

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When his boss goes on holiday, Sid assumes responsibility for guarding his property - but has trouble enforcing his regime. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland
