programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A vast new island in the seas off Dubai, which has been created to house the world's biggest observation wheel - twice the size of the London Eye

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Whistler on the Canadian Pacific seaboard, a remote mountain town whose creation was made possible with record-breaking engineering equipment, helping establish one of the best ski resorts in the world

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough continues his exploration of the continent in east Africa, which is a haven for life and supports a higher density of large mammals than anywhere else on Earth. From dense forests to snow-capped peaks, steamy swamps to endless savannah, it's also a land of unpredictable extremes, where creatures must adapt to survive. In Tanzania, a tiny lizard risks its life to eat flies from the face of a sleepy lion. The nesting habits of the 4ft shoebill are also captured on camera - the giant bird has two chicks, but due to sibling rivalry only one will survive. On the plains of Amboseli, a baby elephant is near to death in the drought, so its mother leaves the herd to stay with her offspring until the bitter end

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the varied creatures of the Congo rainforest, one of the most competitive habitats on Earth. A sweet-toothed chimpanzee uses a complex array of tools to access honey from a beehive, the elusive picathartes bird feeds unsuspecting frogs to its dinosaur-like chicks and a massive female python reveals her maternal side as she sunbathes to incubate her eggs. Tiny leaf-folding frogs battle it out kung fu style, and as darkness falls the forest elephants gather to face their rivals

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes some of the wildlife spectacles of southern Africa, starting out in a little-explored rainforest discovered less than a decade ago by scientists who spotted it on satellite maps. There, thousands of butterflies dance for the right to mate, while on a tiny island off Mozambique, green turtles emerge from eggs and begin a hazardous journey to the water - with pied crows and hungry crabs eager to get them. Cameras also capture one of the largest gatherings of great white sharks on the planet as 30 of the predators circle around a dead whale

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. The naked mole rat avoids the heat by living a subterranean existence, while the silver ant is able to take on the midday sun - but needs a 'spacesuit' to do it

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels along Brunel's Great Western Railway, learning how mass tourism started in Swindon, before visiting Bath to discover the history of the famous spa baths. His last stop is Bristol, where he tries his hand at blowing blue glass

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Swindon to Penzance. Starting in Yatton, he travels along a line that was once used to transport fresh strawberries across the country, but was closed by Dr Beeching in the 1960s. He also visits the Gough Cave in Cheddar Gorge to view the remains of the 9,000-year-old Cheddar Man - the oldest complete skeleton in Britain

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave gets a nasty surprise when he opens the bonnet of a fast Ford from the 80's, and an Austin Heavy 12 nearly a century old sets off a bidding war at the auction

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn goes behind the scenes at London's King's Cross station and finds out about the disused funicular railway hidden in the Avon Gorge in Bristol, blasted out of the cliff

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Forgotten relics from the defence of America's homeland, concealed by the banks of the Mississippi, and a concrete behemoth on the French coast that proved invulnerable to attacks

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A look at some of Switzerland's 300,000 subterranean bunkers, built across history. Plus, the extraordinary creation of a Liverpool entrepreneur that's shrouded in mystery, and a network of man-made tunnels used by adventurous subterranean thrill-seekers deep beneath Slovenia's landscape

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A vast new island in the seas off Dubai, which has been created to house the world's biggest observation wheel - twice the size of the London Eye

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Whistler on the Canadian Pacific seaboard, a remote mountain town whose creation was made possible with record-breaking engineering equipment, helping establish one of the best ski resorts in the world

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Nato's Airborne Warning and Control System, known as Awac, a cutting-edge aircraft with a huge radar dome mounted on its roof that enables pilots to spot hostile intrusion as they protect state borders of the countries in the Nato alliance. One of the most complex surveillance aircrafts in the world, it is able to seek out, locate, and defend against threats - no matter how fast moving or well hidden

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The battle for North Africa, which involved three years of fighting between Rommel's Afrika Korps and Montgomery's Desert Rats and culminated in the Allied victory at El Alamein. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the myth of German invincibility was finally debunked by their defeat at the Battle of Stalingrad. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Account of Operation Barbarossa, Germany's plan to invade the Soviet Union in 1941, revealing the massive human and financial cost endured by the Soviet people, who fought a bloody and lonely war with little support from the Allies. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: British bombers are unable to hit their targets precisely and attack German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An American prison for the violent and deranged with links to an infamous cult leader, and a cavernous tunnel network in France offering shelter from unrelenting bombardment

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On his journey from Swindon to Penzance, Michael Portillo explores Torquay, where he meets meteorologist David Braine to find out whether the town is Britain's warmest spot. He then takes a trip on the Paignton and Dartmouth Steam Railway, and spends the night at the Royal Castle Hotel in Dartmouth, where he learns about the town's naval past. After sailing up the River Dart he arrives in Totnes, and discovers the community's efforts to become more environmentally friendly

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo spends the night in a converted post office railway carriage before continuing his journey from Swindon to Penzance, heading for Par, where he meets miner Ivor Bowditch to explore the largest clay mine in the world. In Mevagissey he discovers how the methods for pilchard fishing have changed over the years, and on a visit to the Lost Gardens of Heligan, Michael learns about the 1,000-acre estate's history

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack heads to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference, and luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what's been brought in for auction

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek, with help from the Mathewson clan, drag a mini pickup out of the barn where it's been for 20 years, and a 350cc Douglas Mark V motorbike from 1950 goes under the hammer

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits Ribblehead Viaduct in the Yorkshire Dales, talks to the man who saved it from closure and tours the breathtaking stations of the Hungerbergbahn in Innsbruck

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Albanian village home to Allied secret agents, devastated for resisting the Nazi war machine, and a notorious asylum in America that enforced lobotomies

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The Wieliczka Salt mine in southern Poland, some 327 metres deep, where the voids left behind from excavating salt have been transformed into extraordinary spaces, carved by hand

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Forbidden History

Jamie Theakston looks beyond the recognised orthodoxies and beliefs as he goes - literally and figuratively - underground to explore the extraordinary history of the Church of Satan

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How German U-boats were used to disrupt the flow of goods from America across the Atlantic Ocean in an attempt to starve Britain into submission

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.desc: In Devon, Drew Pritchard and Paul Cowland fall for the Gallic charm of a Renault 4. It's a rare limited-edition model, but the duo need to replicate its unique interior fabric

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: Paul Jr and Paul Sr have a meeting away from the cameras to discuss their relationship, while OCC finishes a bike for MyPillow and PJD begins a build for a baseball player

programme.title: Aircrash Confidential
programme.desc: Documentary series, using expert opinions and CGI to investigate the causes of aviation disasters, and the steps being taken to prevent future accidents

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: Exploring the Beluga Bremen, the world's first multi-purpose heavy-lift cargo carrier

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: A look at how tea, roof finials, artificial flowers and alloy wheels are either made or processed

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: How gel caps, playground spring riders, frozen pancakes and natural rubber are made

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Somerset local Dean Edwards takes on another celebrity chef in the culinary contest, with dishes including Mizo-glazed aubergine, and chicken and artichoke ravioli

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers take on two tough challenges set by judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles, including a messy Technical Challenge working with sticky swirls of dough

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows five properties to Rachel and Miguel, who have sold their house in Dorset and are now planning a permanent move to Spain's highly sought-after Orihuela Costa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular sunny European locations. Potential buyers explore the stunning Croatian island of Hvar

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer and fancy-dress company owner Julia Knight kicks off the competition in Derby. In a bid to win the £1,000 prize, she introduces a Mexican theme, complete with chilli, tortillas, ponchos, sombreros and fake moustaches

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Postman Graham Fox plays host in Derby, hoping his food can reduce the tension following Michele Cooper and Julia Knight's row. The evening is going well until one of the guests finds a piece of crab shell in a fish cake, and by the time the bread and butter pudding is served, the arguing has started again

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holistic therapist Patsy Sewell-Mwamba rustles up an exotic feast of Caribbean dishes in Derby. The diners dress up to the nines for the occasion, but Julia Knight puts a damper on things by refusing to eat dessert. She is also challenged to try her first oyster - much to the amusement of the other guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Erotic-dance teacher Michele Cooper hosts the next party in Derby, hoping her savoury cheesecake, chicken pie and apple tarte Tatin will satisfy her guests' tastebuds. However, the rivalry between her and Julia Knight threatens to ruin the evening, as the picky eater tries to hide food in her handbag

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host in Derby is web designer Danny Fletcher, who perplexes his guests by handing them a menu written in French. He also tries to impress with a chicken terrine wrapped in Parma ham, followed by duck a l'orange - but things start to go wrong when he burns the potatoes

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer informs on Krusty the Clown for apparently robbing the Kwik-E-Mart, but Bart is convinced of his hero's innocence and tries to clear his name. With the voice of Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart tries to impress his tough new friends by decapitating a statue of the town's founder Jebediah Springfield, but his act of vandalism only succeeds in turning everybody against him

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting Windsor to meet Pete and Phil, who rent out cars to the film and TV business. Henry finds an Austrian moped which is missing an engine, while Simon picks a fibreglass suit of armour

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Glasgow couple Colin and Richard, who have a £200,000 budget to purchase a property in the resort of Playa del Ingles on Gran Canaria

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the festive season in Devon and Cornwall with people whose livelihood depends on this busy time of year. The first stop is Cornwall's Lizard peninsula, to meet the Richards family, who have been farming this region for more than 500 years. Cameras also follow Devon artist Luna North, whose love of the region inspires her to paint her own Christmas card

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A chance to catch-up with some of the best moments from this year's episodes, featuring some of the country's favourite celebrities. The line-up critiquing the year's biggest and best shows include Denise van Outen and her partner Eddie, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mum Linda, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, KSI and S-X, and Shaun Ryder and Bez

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The comedians gather for the grand final, to find which one of them will be going home with Taskmaster Greg Davies' golden head - a prize that lies within the grasp of four of them. But there are still more games to be played, leading to Katherine Parkinson searching for a spider, Daisy May Cooper destroying a mannequin and Johnny Vegas's pocket watch finally coming in handy

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix and his team head for the British countryside to celebrate love in the most festive of locations, where 28-year-old Holly goes on a date with 32-year-old Shane. Elsewhere, Paul, who has never had a boyfriend, is set up with 35-year-old IT worker Alex from London, and Shauna and James, who are both from Dewsbury, enjoy a festive date

programme.title: The Crystal Maze
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade guides families through the four zones of the Maze, tackling a range of skill, mystery, physical and mental challenges

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere and Warwick Castles
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: Royal Antiques Revived
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell meets three royal repairers as they embark on a journey of incredible restoration. From King George IV's piano to a car that's believed to have belonged to the Queen Mother, and a very rare copy of King Louis XV roll-top desk, every item provides a glimpse into the lives of the people who owned them and the restorers working on them

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George is joined by designer, craftsman and maker Will Hardie on trips to Norway, Finland, the Alps and Canada to check out some amazing winter designs - from ice hotels to stunning modern homes. Along the way, the duo build an igloo, race toboggans, feed reindeer and dodge a bear

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking at Christmas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jamie Oliver looks at ways to make gifts to share with loved ones, including roasted mulled wine ham that's cooked and glazed in all the flavours of Christmas. Accompanied by cheeses you can flavour and deliver ready to be baked. Jamie and the children also create the ultimate yule log for their grandparents

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Somerset local Dean Edwards takes on another celebrity chef in the culinary contest, with the rivals sourcing all their ingredients from a corner shop. Hosted by Chizzy Akudolu

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers show off their dessert skills, including Franken-Desserts - a mash-up of two puddings that would never usually be seen sharing the same bowl. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kamlesh and Sukhwinder have been married for 42 years, and now their four children have flown the nest, wish to buy a winter home in southern Alicante, with its breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and lively atmosphere. Jean Johansson shows them five properties for their £95,000 budget, but whether any will take their fancy remains to be seen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular sunny European locations. Potential buyers explore Lucca in Italy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lady of luxury Tanya Jay hosts the first dinner party in Kent, hoping to impress her guests with an ambitious 17-dish menu accompanied by ample amounts of alcohol - but businessman Allen Brockington does not react well to the spicy food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former punk rocker and die-hard Arsenal fan Trevor Smith hosts the second dinner party in Kent, but the confident joker soon finds himself struggling in the kitchen and calls on his wife Bev to help him get the food ready in time for his guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Allen Brockington hosts the third meal of the week in Kent, showing surprising culinary skill concealed behind a laid-back exterior. However, having a menu of restaurant favourites may be more than he can handle - allowing Trevor Smith to get his revenge for the teasing he had to endure the night before

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Laid-back market researcher Zoe Mortlock aims to impress her guests in Kent by cooking a three-course meal influenced by her travels around the world. Her only concern is fussy eater Tanya Jay, who is yet to leave an empty plate so far this week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Super-confident Steven Parks hosts the final meal in Kent, sure his menu of local produce will help him ensure victory is in the bag. However, the fish turns out to be undercooked and has to be removed from the dinner table and put back in the oven

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes Marge for granted once too often, driving her into the arms of a smooth-talking Lothario who charms her at the local bowling alley. Animated comedy, with the guest voice of Albert Brooks

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is photographed in a compromising situation with an exotic dancer during a workmate's stag party and finds to his dismay that Marge is in no mood to forgive and forget

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family-run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo head for Crewe to meet rock and roller Billy, who lives in a house that is frozen in time in the 1950s

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a Gloucestershire couple find a holiday home in Fuerteventura that is large enough to accommodate three generations of their family

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Sarah and Graham recruit a band of workers to begin Covid-safe renovation work after a month's pause in the project. The couple disagree on whether they should have animals, so arrange to borrow four alpacas from a nearby farm to see if they like the idea. The farmhouse finally has power, but it still needs running water, so plans get underway to dig a borehole

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere and Warwick Castles
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: A Christmas Carol (1984)
programme.desc: Miser Ebenezer Scrooge is given a Christmas Eve to remember when a quartet of ghosts - his dead business partner and the spirits of Christmases past, present and future - turn up determined to cure him of his money-grubbing ways. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' classic, starring George C Scott, Nigel Davenport, Frank Finlay, Angela Pleasence, David Warner, Edward Woodward and Susannah York

programme.title: The Man Who Invented Christmas (2017)
programme.desc: Struggling with debt, Charles Dickens begins work on a new book entitled A Christmas Carol. Comedy drama, starring Dan Stevens

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Manchester to explore the spirit of protest and revolution that grew out of the experience of Britain's working class during the Industrial Revolution. At the John Ryland Library, Alice is granted access to rarely-seen period documents that highlight the true human cost of the Peterloo massacre. At the Free Trade Hall, she explores how Manchester found itself at the heart of the battle against the hated Corn Laws, and at Chetham's Library, Roberts sits at the very desk where Friedrich Engels and Karl Marx, inspired by the living conditions of Manchester's working poor, began the discussions and research that would lead to the publication of the Communist Manifesto

programme.title: Inside John Lewis: Trouble at the Tills
programme.desc: John Lewis has been one of the most iconic names on the British high street for more than a century. But 2020 has been devastating. Steph McGovern investigates challenges facing the company, and what they tell us about the wider future of our high streets as more of us turn to online shopping

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is fired from the power plant and becomes so dejected that he contemplates suicide - until a near-tragic road accident makes him realise his purpose in life is to make Springfield safer

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu hosts the contest in which well-known chefs compete against a talented local while having to source all ingredients for their two-course menu on a budget of £10. In this final edition, Dean Edwards summons all his experience, uses all his skill and finds a bit of good luck to try to defeat the competition

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers need to impress judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles with their pastry-plaiting skills, in a challenge that offers the contenders of Heat A their last chance to go through to Finals week. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire fell in love with Bulgaria 30 years ago when she was working as a teacher, and now she can't wait to buy a holiday home there. She loves the traditional town of Tryavna's authentic atmosphere and beautiful countryside, and is after a three-bedroom house for her budget of £40,000. Jonnie Irwin shows Sarah and her best friend Suzie five properties to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Gary and Susie, who want to start a new adventure in Florida's beautiful Sarasota Keys. For their budget of £220,000 they're after a place to escape the British winters, as close to the amazing beaches of the Gulf of Mexico as possible. Jonnie shows them how far their budget will stretch in this highly desirable corner of the US

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: White witch Maureen McKissock, whose house contains books of spells, shelves full of crystals and an earless cat, kicks off the competition in Ayr by preparing a curry with a potent cocktail of spices. However, one of the guests voices her objections to the main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: College lecturer Elaine Munro hosts the second party in Ayr, hoping her culinary creation of Scottish salmon wrapped in Parma ham is an improvement on Maureen McKissock's spicy offerings. She also tries to impress by offering the guests champagne on their arrival - but businessman Forbes Robertson decides he would prefer a cup of tea

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Casino owner Forbes Robertson takes a gamble by serving up pigs' trotters which cost just 30p each. He splashes out on champagne, hoping to put his guests in high spirits, but it remains to be seen whether his down-to-earth choice will satisfy his diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Lizzy McCubbin hosts an upmarket dinner party in Ayr and plans to impress her guests with a lavish four-course meal and first-class presentation. In a bid to stop smokers Elaine Munro and Sarah Pickering from leaving the table for cigarette breaks, she presents them with a gift of nicotine gum, but the gesture is not well received

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nature-lover Sarah Pickering hosts the final dinner party of the week in Ayr, with a menu featuring soup, home-made ice-cream and a flying Scotsman - chicken stuffed with haggis and wrapped in Ayrshire bacon. The meal takes a lot of preparation, allowing the guests ample time to consider what score they will give

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is sent to France on a student exchange, where he is used for slave labour by corrupt wine-makers. Back in Springfield, his Albanian replacement takes an unhealthy interest in the nuclear power plant

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart inadvertently exposes Krusty the Clown as one of the worst tax-dodgers in history and faces the trauma of seeing his TV idol prosecuted for fraud

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet a woman whose family have raced cars for three generations, and Simon comes across an old ship's carpenter's chest he can restore

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: The Winners
programme.desc: The second of two specials paying tribute to the winners from past shows, made to celebrate the series' 10th anniversary. The second programme focuses on the five most recent winners, including Nadiya, Candice and Rahul, reliving their most memorable moments, from their first Signature challenge to their final Showstopper

programme.title: One Night In Hamleys
programme.desc: Three comedians get to live out their childhood fantasies by spending the night in London's famous toy shop, with no security guards to keep them out of mischief. The comics face a series of ridiculous festive games and challenges with only two rules - nobody is allowed to go to sleep and anything they break, they have to pay for

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the words and numbers game, with Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican face off against Sean Lock and David Mitchell, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick, Angel, Dorothy and Arthur, as well as Angel's parents Jenni and Steve prepare to celebrate a family Christmas in France. With an old 19th-century chateau ledger found in the attic and classic cookbooks to hand, the Strawbridges take inspiration from Christmases past, discovering what food, festivities, gifts and games would have been like in bygone eras. Dick and Angel's imagination goes into overdrive as they create a winter wonderland with an ice rink at its heart. Later, as the family settles down to dinner, so the sound of traditional hand bells rings out across the chateau and they wonder if their exploration of the past will create chateau traditions for the future

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere and Warwick Castles
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix pits a leading chocolatier against a virtuoso pastry chef to replicate one of the nation's best-loved festive chocolate brands - Quality Street. As ever, the chefs must work out the secret techniques and recipes behind the household favourites before creating their own versions to be judged by a panel of Quality Street insiders and experts. But with so many to choose from, will they be expected to remake the green triangle, the orange crunch or the iconic purple one?

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Sarah and Graham recruit a band of workers to begin Covid-safe renovation work after a month's pause in the project. The couple disagree on whether they should have animals, so arrange to borrow four alpacas from a nearby farm to see if they like the idea. The farmhouse finally has power, but it still needs running water, so plans get underway to dig a borehole

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: The Winners
programme.desc: The second of two specials paying tribute to the winners from past shows, made to celebrate the series' 10th anniversary. The second programme focuses on the five most recent winners, including Nadiya, Candice and Rahul, reliving their most memorable moments, from their first Signature challenge to their final Showstopper

programme.title: The Tiger Who Came to Tea
programme.desc: Hand-drawn animated version of Judith Kerr's much-loved tale, which celebrated its 50th anniversary in 2018. A tea-guzzling tiger turns up unannounced and eats and drinks young Sophie and her mummy out of everything in the house, even draining the water from their taps. Narrated by David Walliams, with David Oyelowo providing the voice for the big cat alongside seven-year-old Clara Ross as Sophie, with other characters played by Benedict Cumberbatch, Tamsin Greig and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old TJ from Folkestone weighs in at over 19 stone, and is worried that if he doesn't act now, his younger brothers will follow in his footsteps

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie and Mia begin to make wedding arrangements, but things fall apart when their families meet, leading them to consider running off and marrying in Las Vegas. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie returns to his bachelor lifestyle, while Alan is depressed following his divorce from Kandi. Starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie finally tells Alan why he and Mia broke up - she had wanted the playboy to evict his brother and nephew from the house. The pair also deal with a noisy neighbour

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A dispute involving a female student and the man living upstairs descends into violence, while Doug and Mavis moved into a new house with their menagerie of animals and were stunned when a neighbour complained about the noise, but that turned out to be just the beginning of their troubles

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Staffordshire, the agents chase up a £3,000 deposit owed by a landlord to his tenant, and officers in Birmingham try to recover £25,000 from a nightclub owner for unpaid solicitor fees. Meanwhile, agents Max and Paul are in Surrey as they attempt to collect more than £3,000 owed to a garage after a customer disputed the repairs made to his van

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Officers chase £4,500 in court fees in north Wales after a property deal turned sour, and in Birmingham the agents try to collect over £3,500 from a single mum who defaulted on loan repayments. Elsewhere in Worcestershire, the enforcement agents try to recover more than £10,000 from a shop-keeper for fraudulently keeping a deposit during a property sale

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Staffordshire agents try to collect nearly £2,500 in unpaid parking fines, while officers Matt and Garry head to the West Midlands to recover £4,500 owed from a debtor to his former girlfriend. Agents travel to south-east London looking to collect £3,000 owed by a taxi company

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south-east London, officers Gary and Cona chase a debt of nearly £50,000 in unpaid rent, leading to a feisty confrontation with the debtor. Agents Gary and Paul try to recover over £2,000 owed by a teenager to a local garage in Kent, and officers Max and Paul head to south-west London on the trail of a film producer who owes £8,000 to financial advisers

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The team carries out a raid that turns up a stash of drugs, a weapon and a large pile of cash, while Jon Peacock is amazed to encounter a half-naked man covered in blood on the wintry streets of Lincoln, but the real surprise comes when the officer finds out what he has been up to. Suspicious driving leads Jim Woolerton to discover super-strength lager and drugs, and a speeding motorist comes out with a very middle-class excuse after being clocked travelling at 110mph

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Brian Conville gives a man a dressing-down for dangerous driving on the school run, and Barry Peach's detective skills come to the fore when he apprehends a fugitive who has been using a false identity. Meanwhile, during an initiative to clamp down on rural crime, officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit find themselves pursuing some stolen tractors

programme.title: Charlie's Angels: Full Throttle (2003)
programme.desc: The high-kicking trio return, this time to hunt down the villains responsible for stealing two encoded rings containing top-secret information. The case brings them into conflict with a former Angel gone bad, a spurned ex-lover out for revenge and their creepy old enemy the Thin Man. Action adventure sequel, starring Drew Barrymore, Cameron Diaz and Lucy Liu, with Demi Moore, John Cleese and Bernie Mac

Plus, a look at the history of Quality Street and Roses, from their origins in Victorian Britain to their sometimes disastrous attempts to branch out into new flavours

programme.title: London Has Fallen (2016)
programme.desc: World leaders gather in London for the funeral of the British Prime Minister, unaware they have been led into a trap. A terrorist plans to kill them all, and devastate the city, and the only people in a position to stop him are the President of the United States and the indomitable head of his secret service. Action thriller sequel to Olympus Has Fallen, starring Gerard Butler, Aaron Eckhart and Morgan Freeman

programme.desc: Jessica's idyllic winter weekend at a ski lodge takes a terrifying turn as her snowbound fellow guests are stalked by a fanatic with murder in mind. Angela Lansbury stars, with a guest appearance by Emma Samms

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica heads to West Virginia to come to the aid of one of her former students, who has been arrested for murder - but the unfriendly locals are less than happy about her investigation. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Based in New York researching her next book, Jessica intervenes when a jeweller is murdered and a famous actress's tiara worth millions is stolen. She establishes a news reporter as the prime suspect. Starring Angela Lansbury, Michael Horton and Jean Peters

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A perfectly preserved plane is discovered in a remote Alaskan ice field, but unfortunately for Jessica, investigators believe it was the scene of a crime committed by her late husband in 1952. Richard Roundtree guest stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits a friend's Arizona ranch - but as soon as she arrives, the community is plunged into disarray by a local psychic's sinister predictions. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is delighted to run into an old friend while on a shopping expedition in Boston - until he unaccountably denies knowing her. She recruits her dependable ally Harry McGraw to help solve the mystery. Jerry Orbach guest stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica takes time out from writing to visit her nephew - but soon has to put on her thinking cap to solve the murder of a TV sports personality. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury and Paul Sorvino

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jessica receives a visit from Eudora McVeigh, a rival mystery writer with seemingly friendly intentions - but the discovery of a corpse in Cabot Cove leads her to suspect the woman of sinister motives. Starring Angela Lansbury and Jean Simmons

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Seth lies critically ill in hospital, the doctor reveals he was poisoned, but Jessica suspects someone else was the intended victim and decides to confront Eudora. Jean Simmons guest stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote: The Celtic Riddle (2003)
programme.desc: Sleuth Jessica Fletcher heads to Ireland for the reading of an old friend's will, but is intrigued to find it contains a series of cryptic clues. Certain they point to hidden treasure, and are in some way linked to a spate of unexplained deaths, she enlists the help of the deceased's family to solve the puzzle. Mystery, starring Angela Lansbury, Fionnula Flanagan, Sarah-Jane Potts and Peter Donat

Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and another officer team up to track down a serial killer, focusing their investigation on a recently released sex offender - but fresh evidence leads them to believe he may have been wrongly convicted. Concerned that a miscarriage of justice could have occurred, they look over old case files and turn their attention to the suspect's neighbour, a man with employment links in the vicinity of seven murders around the country. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and David Keith

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a man is shot outside a gay club, the detectives' investigations lead to his family, where they discover the wife of the dead man has a turbulent past. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

However, after receiving news that Olinsky has been stabbed, Voight seeks justice - especially when the prison CCTV footage of an earlier altercation shows a security guard turning a blind eye to proceedings. The guard is squeezed for a lead into Olinsky's killer, who is quickly identified and has his paymaster's name beaten out of him. Woods finds a new witness who says she saw Voight visit Bingham on the day of his murder. After some financial temptation, she agrees to go along with the plan and say that she saw Voight put a gun to Bingham's head

programme.desc: Paramedics with the East Anglian Air Ambulance are called to the scene of an accident, where a driver must be airlifted to hospital after being cut from the wreckage of his car. Plus, Norfolk police race to a siege involving a man threatening violence

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Police in King's Lynn deal with a difficult Saturday night, in which they are assaulted and a man resists arrest. In Norwich, firefighters cut off the roof of a car to free the driver trapped inside after a road accident

programme.title: 999 Frontline
programme.desc: Crime scene investigators collect forensic evidence in Luton, while in Chelmsford, paramedics attend to a pregnant woman with severe abdominal pains, before taking her to hospital for further treatment

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Series following the work of Britain's police dogs and their handlers on armed raids and sieges. Officers with the Metropolitan Police Dog Unit use sniffer spaniels as they conduct daytime drug searches on the streets of Camden, and Alsatians track and attack armed robbers at night. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: An insight into the work of the police dogs of the West Yorkshire force as they hunt for drugs and track down burglars. Also featured are two of Europe's most famous canine detectives, Eddie and Keela - formerly employed by South Yorkshire Police - whose skills have been used in the hunt for Madeleine McCann and the investigation into allegations of child abuse in Jersey. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: The work of British Transport Police's canine units, which help monitor the safety of commuters on thousands of miles of railway and underground track. The programme follows a spaniel trained to search for drugs, as well as police dogs that help their human handlers control the crowds at football matches. Ken Stott narrates

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: A focus on the police canine unit in West Yorkshire, home to hard-working sniffer dog Bella and expert tracker Otto - who is on the hunt for an escaped car thief. Beth the German Shepherd is taken on an emergency trip to the vet and there is a profile of a dog capable of catching killers. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Cocker spaniel Diesel helps his handler PC Adele Gibson and the Territorial Support Group raids a suspected drug den. Sergeant Pete Madden and his German shepherd Brodie are on the trail of car thieves, while Paddy the English spaniel takes to the water with his companion PC John Lane

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: PC Rogers reveals how he and Alsatian Leo help clear the streets of car thieves, and the officer comes up against a man who threatens to shoot him as the Greater Manchester Police dog squad tackles more criminals. A drunk driver takes on PC Greaves and his sidekick Riley, while spaniel Colin - who is trained to deal with explosives - and PC Carlin are put on the front line when a member of the Cabinet arrives in the city. Ken Stott narrates

programme.title: Rugby's Funniest Moments

programme.title: Premier League's Greatest Moments

programme.title: Micky Flanagan's Christmas
programme.desc: The comedian investigates Christmas, visiting a tinsel factory and finding out why people do unspeakable things at their office parties, as well as shares his take on 2018

programme.title: Greg Davies: The Back of My Mum's Head
programme.desc: Stand-up comedy with the Man Down star, recorded at the Lowry in Salford. Stung by his mother's suggestion that he is 'not normal', Greg puts everyone else under the microscope in a petty quest to discover if anyone actually is. The tour received rave reviews and was extended to 94 dates, including multiple sell-out nights at London's Hammersmith Apollo and Southbank Centre

programme.desc: Ten years ago, in November 2006, Al Jazeera English was launched. To mark that anniversary, we've created a new show, REWIND, which over the coming weeks will let us re-screen and update some of the channel's most memorable and award-winning documentaries of the past decade. Filmed against the dramatic backdrop of Doha's iconic Museum of Islamic Art, our team will be finding out what happened to some of the characters in those programmes and asking how their stories have changed in the years since our cameras left

programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist is joined by Toots Hibbert, frontman of reggae band Toots and the Maytals, who discusses his formative years in Jamaica and some of his favourite records

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow experiences what New York was like at the height of the Industrial Revolution. Millions fled to escape the persecution, poverty and famine of 19th-century Europe, only to be confronted by living conditions worse than those they had left behind. He explores the overcrowded slums of Lower Manhattan, which were a breeding ground for cholera and typhoid, and has a go at cooking using clothes dye and floor cleaner - ingredients that were added to meals to disguise the smell of spoiled meat. He also discovers how certain feats of engineering transformed not only the city, but the world

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef explores the capital's fast-food scene, and demonstrates how one quick bread recipe can cater for breakfast, lunch and dinner

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Observational documentary revealing the inner workings of Oxford Street, following police and paramedics as they keep the public safe, as well as the staff in the shops and on the underground

programme.title: Miracle in Soho (1957)
programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. Drama, based on the novel by Mary Hayley Bell, starring Alan Bates, Hayley Mills, Bernard Lee and Norman Bird

programme.title: Oxford Street
programme.desc: A Montage of the famous street around Christmas time in 1970, shot on 16mm film

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The guitarist talks to his current Faces bandmate Mick Hucknall. The duo perform a version of Bob Dylan's One of Us Must Know, and Ronnie shares his memories of Jimi Hendrix

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef explores the capital's fast-food scene, and demonstrates how one quick bread recipe can cater for breakfast, lunch and dinner

programme.title: Dam Busters
programme.desc: The stories behind one of the most daring raids of the Second World War, in which a squadron of Lancaster bombers destroyed two dams in the heart of industrial Germany

programme.title: A Jubilee Tribute to the Queen by the Prince of Wales
programme.desc: Prince Charles pays a personal tribute to Her Majesty to mark her 60-year reign. He reflects on various public events and private family moments over the past six decades and shows previously unseen photographs and home movies - some of which were shot by the Queen herself

programme.title: All You Need Is Love
programme.desc: Landmark series from the 1970s, telling the history of folk and protest music. Featuring interviews and footage of artists including Joan Baez, Woody Guthrie and Leonard Cohen

programme.desc: Series of one-off comedy dramas

programme.title: Damage (1992)
programme.desc: An English politician at the height of his career risks everything he holds dear when he gives in to his impulses and recklessly embarks on a passionate relationship with his son's girlfriend. When news of the affair reaches Whitehall, the fallout looks set to end his political ambitions. Drama, starring Jeremy Irons, Juliette Binoche, Rupert Graves, and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: Triggermen (2002)
programme.desc: Two small-time British con-men are mistaken for professional killers and hired to assassinate a Chicago crime boss - a situation they are quick to exploit. Comedy, starring Donnie Wahlberg, Michael Rapaport and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike is having financial trouble, so Laura decides to help out. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Michael Williams

programme.title: A Fine Romance
programme.desc: Mike's van and where he can park it interferes in the development of his relationship with Laura. Judi Dench and Michael Williams star

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The Drakkar drifts down river towards a waterfall, and the Vikings must give chase on horses to save it

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar and Vic go in search of a magic sword, unaware that an evil warlord plans to steal it from them once they have it

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The band suffers a run of mishaps while in Acapulco, and discovers that a shaman has accidentally cursed them with bad luck

programme.title: Dot and Spot
programme.desc: Santa needs the help of Dot and Spot, two Dalmatian puppies, on Christmas Eve

programme.title: Snow White
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. Snow White finds herself in the company of seven trusty dwarfs after her evil stepmother attempts her revenge on her

programme.title: Cup Fever (1965)
programme.desc: A crooked councillor tries to make life difficult for a children's football team, so they will lose a match against his son's side. However, help is at hand from Manchester United manager Matt Busby. Children's drama, starring David Lodge and Busby as himself, with cameos from a host of football stars

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Zoe's divorce comes through, and while Alec is keen to celebrate, she is not so sure

programme.title: Untamed Romania
programme.desc: In Romania, ancient forests and expansive wetlands provide habitats to some of Europe's most iconic creatures. This is a celebration of nature, in all its beauty and diversity

programme.title: Doctor in the House (1954)
programme.desc: A medical student is taken under the wing of three roguish colleagues who lead him astray into a series of pranks and amorous escapades that succeed in infuriating the dictatorial chief surgeon. Comedy based on the novel by Richard Gordon, starring Dirk Bogarde, Kenneth More, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Doctor at Large (1957)
programme.desc: Newly qualified doctor Simon Sparrow leaves St Swithin's Hospital and relocates to the countryside in search of a job at two prospering practices. However, before long he runs into fearsome former mentor Sir Lancelot Spratt. Comedy, starring Dirk Bogarde, James Robertson Justice and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Passport to Pimlico (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a London suburb discover documents proving their neighbourhood is not part of Britain, and declare independence. Appointing their own head of state, they begin erecting borders as the government resorts to desperate measures to bring the community back under British rule. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway and Margaret Rutherford

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: The English king leads his armies into war to reclaim control of France - culminating in a pivotal confrontation with much larger enemy forces on the fields of Agincourt. Adaptation of Shakespeare's historical play, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Derek Jacobi, Brian Blessed, Emma Thompson, Ian Holm, Robert Stephens and a young Christian Bale

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Pretentious Alan, violent Kix and sex-mad Eleanor are part of a 1960s group of poets, artists and angry young men who convene for a weekend of amusement at a large villa overlooking the sea. Comedy, starring Peter Richardson, Adrian Edmondson, Dawn French, Jennifer Saunders and Rik Mayall

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Series of one-off comedy dramas

programme.title: The Legend of Barney Thomson (2015)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered Glasgow barber finds his mundane life turned upside down when he accidentally commits murder. Soon one killing leads to another, and his desperate efforts to cover up his crimes leads him to uncover his mother's dark secrets. Black comedy based on the series of books by Douglas Lindsay, directed by and starring Robert Carlyle, with Emma Thompson and Ray Winstone

programme.title: The Exception (2016)
programme.desc: A German soldier tries to determine if the Dutch resistance has planted a spy to infiltrate the home of Kaiser Wilhelm in Holland during the onset of the Second World War, but falls for a young Jewish-Dutch woman during his investigation. Drama, starring Lily James, Jai Courtney and Christopher Plummer

programme.title: Helga, She Wolf of Stilberg (1978)
programme.desc: A sadistic female Nazi officer runs a women's prison during the Second World War, and forces the inmates to be her sexual playthings. However, the latest arrival, a determined resistance fighter, proves to be far more difficult to dominate. Prison drama, starring Malisa Longo and Patrizia Gori. In French

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Zoe's divorce comes through, and while Alec is keen to celebrate, she is not so sure

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Featuring inspirational walks from around Britain

programme.title: Christopher and Holly
programme.desc: Two teddy bears attempt to save Christmas for two children

programme.title: Nick and Noel
programme.desc: Animated Christmas Special about a girl who wants a mother for Christmas and the cat and dog who make her wish come true

programme.title: Fairy Tales for You
programme.desc: An animated version of Rapunzel, read by Bobby Davro

programme.title: Nanny McPhee
programme.desc: Production by the London Children's Ballet. A father with seven badly behaved children is in desperate need of some discipline and then a curious stranger comes into their lives

programme.title: The Battle of Billy's Pond (1976)
programme.desc: A boy investigates after his cat becomes ill after eating a dead fish from a local pond. He discovers the fish was killed by industrial waste that a company has been dumping in the water - but the owners will go to any lengths to keep their misdeeds a secret. Family drama, starring Ben Buckton and Andrew Ashby

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec gets on so well with Zoe's parents that she begins to feel left out

programme.title: Gracias Choir Christmas Concert
programme.desc: A special concert of Christmas Carols and Hymns from the Gracias Choir - an award winning choir founded in South Korea

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A journey along the Thames from the Cotswold Hills to the North Sea, concentrating on the lives of the water voles, swans and eels that call it home, and highlighting the escalating battle to control the waterways despite technological advances in flood management. As London continues to sink faster than Venice, the untamed nature of the river is revealed

programme.title: Kind Hearts and Coronets (1949)
programme.desc: The penniless grandson of a duke plots to eliminate the eight relatives standing between him and a vast inheritance. He shoots, drowns, poisons and explodes his way through his family tree, but his final triumph is ruined when his lady love's husband kills himself. Ealing black comedy, starring Alec Guinness as all eight potential victims, with Dennis Price, Valerie Hobson and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: Are You Being Served? (1977)
programme.desc: Havoc descends on the coast of Spain as the motley Grace Brothers crew arrive for their annual holiday on the Costa Plonka, which does not exactly live up to the brochure's promise of rest and relaxation. Comedy based on the TV series, starring John Inman, Wendy Richard, Mollie Sugden, Frank Thornton, Trevor Bannister and Arthur English

programme.title: Buttons: A Christmas Tale (2018)
programme.desc: An orphan facing the propspect of life on the street receives a visit from a guardian angel, who comforts her with the story of another child's search for a family and the role another mysterious helper played in her life. Children's fantasy, starring Angela Lansbury and Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Oxford Street
programme.desc: A Montage of the famous street around Christmas time in 1970, shot on 16mm film

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank.

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles (2002)
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson set out to protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskerville family, who believes he is being persecuted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the bleak Devon moors. Mystery, starring Richard Roxburgh, Ian Hart, Richard E Grant, John Nettles, Geraldine James, Matt Day and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Cardboard Gangsters (2017)
programme.desc: A gang of lads selling drugs in Darndale in Dublin's Northside sets out to gain more money and power and enter the big leagues of the narcotics trade. Things begin to unravel quickly as not everyone in the area is willing to let them achieve the notoriety they crave without a fight. Thriller, starring John Connors and Fionn Walton

programme.title: Damage (1992)
programme.desc: An English politician at the height of his career risks everything he holds dear when he gives in to his impulses and recklessly embarks on a passionate relationship with his son's girlfriend. When news of the affair reaches Whitehall, the fallout looks set to end his political ambitions. Drama, starring Jeremy Irons, Juliette Binoche, Rupert Graves, and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Emmanuelle in Soho (1981)
programme.desc: A woman seeking fame and fortune in Soho plays a pivotal role in the struggling marriage of a photographer and his wife, and conspires with them on a plot to blackmail a pornography producer. Erotic drama, starring Angie Quick and Julie Lee

programme.title: World Darts Championship
programme.desc: The afternoon session on day nine of this year's tournament. This stage last year saw Fallon Sherrock shock the darts world with a 3-1 win over Austrian Mensur Suljovic in the second round. That win was Sherrock's second victory in the tournament, after her history-making win over Ted Evetts. Other winners at this stage last year were Australia's Simon Whitlock, and Englishmen Adrian Lewis, Daryl Gurney and Glen Durrant

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the opening day's play in the Fourth Ashes Test, which took place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. This venue was the setting for the First Test back in 2013, and day one saw a total of 14 wickets fall - England were bowled out for 215 in their first innings and the tourists ended the day on 75/4 in reply. Peter Siddle was the pick of the Australian bowlers, taking 5/50 in 14 overs. No English batsman passed 50, with Jonathan Trott coming closest on 48 before being bowled by Siddle

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day five of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the opening contest of the five-match series was brought to a conclusion. Having decided to replace their head coach Mickey Arthur with former batsman Darren Lehmann just 16 days prior to the commencement of this series, Australia will have hoped to claim an unlikely victory in this encounter that could spur them on in the coming Test matches

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Highlights of the opening day of the Fourth Test at Old Trafford as the five-match series continued. The series is level at 1-1 after England recorded a 266-run victory at the Ageas Bowl last time out, which was their first Test victory since August 2013

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Highlights of the third day of the First Test at Headingley, where the three-match series continued

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: South Africa v England. Highlights from day three of the Third Test in the series, held at New Wanderers Stadium in Johannesburg

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v India

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights from the Emirates Durham International Cricket Ground, where the five-match series continued with the fourth day of the Fourth Test. The touring Australians have been beset by problems on and off the pitch since they arrived in this country, beginning with the ICC Champions Trophy which led into this Test series, but put in a much-improved performance in the Third Test. England have already retained the Ashes following the draw at Old Trafford and can afford to concentrate on producing their best cricket, as they aim to outline their superiority over Australia as the nations play back-to-back Test series to avoid a clash with the 2015 World Cup, which will be held in Australia and New Zealand

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided. Brentford agonisingly missed out on automatic promotion on the final day of the regular campaign, but recovered to defeat Swindon Town on penalties in their semi-final, while Yeovil were 2-1 aggregate victors over Sheffield United

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Bradford City v Northampton Town. Action from the 2012/13 League Two play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where promotion to League One awaited the winners. The Bantams were making their second appearance of the season at Wembley having also reached the League Cup final following an outstanding giant-killing run, and they needed to come from behind to edge out Burton Albion to reach this stage. The Cobblers ended a run of recent poor form to win 1-0 in both legs of their semi-final against Cheltenham Town to move within one match of reaching the third tier of English football

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty
